general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or lactating women; inability to use acceptable contraception methods; uncontrolled infections; active hepatitis B or C infection or documented HIV positivity; major surgical procedure or radiotherapy within four weeks preceding study initiation; systemic chemotherapy, radiotherapy, or investigational agents administered within fourteen days (excluding permitted exceptions); symptomatic or untreated leptomeningeal disease or spinal cord compression; New York Heart Association Class III‑IV heart disease, recent myocardial infarction, stroke, or unstable angina within six months; left ventricular ejection fraction < 40%; second primary malignant neoplasm requiring active treatment or without at least two years’ remission (exceptions made for non‑melanoma skin cancer or carcinoma in situ); current anticoagulation with warfarin; intake of potent CYP3A inhibitors or inducers, or citrus fruits (grapefruit, Seville orange, starfruit) within three days of dosing; gastrointestinal malabsorption syndromes preventing oral absorption; any additional severe acute or chronic medical, psychiatric, or laboratory abnormalities judged by investigators to pose undue risk or confound assessment.

general_exclusion_criteria: Inability to give informed consent. Current HIV infection or active hepatitis B surface antigen positivity; untreated hepatitis B core antibody positivity without adequate antiviral prophylaxis; detectable hepatitis C RNA levels. Uncontrolled seizure disorders. Autoimmune central nervous system disease (multiple sclerosis, optic neuritis, etc.) or any CNS disorder judged to impede assessment of neurotoxicity. Significant intracranial findings such as midline shift, elevated cerebrospinal fluid opening pressure, or CSF protein >150 mg/dL. Whole-brain radiation therapy within the preceding three months. Active systemic fungal, viral, or bacterial infection. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Prior or concomitant malignant neoplasm except adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, in‑situ cervical or breast carcinoma cured ≥2 years ago, hormone‑treated breast or prostate cancer in remission ≥2 years, or any solid tumour fully resected/remission ≥2 years. Severe immunosuppression from non‑neurological autoimmune conditions. Requirement for chronic systemic glucocorticoids exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent (up to 2 mg/day dexamethasone allowed for CNS cohort, higher briefly around aphaeresis). Myocardial infarction, coronary intervention, unstable angina, or other serious cardiovascular illness within six months. Concomitant radiotherapy (except palliative low‑dose approaches completed ≤2 weeks before lymphodepletion). Dementia interfering with assessments. Immediate-type hypersensitivity to any investigational product components. Declination to partake in long‑term lentiviral gene‑therapy follow‑up. Prior chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy for any indication. Prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Prior administration of bi‑specific T‑cell engager antibodies. Prior adoptive transfer of T‑cell receptors genetically modified for cancer therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: any ovarian malignant histology. Cancer burden allowed: advanced disease with measurable lesion per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: prior platinum‑containing chemotherapy with documentation of platinum‑refractory or platinum‑resistant disease (disease progressed during or shortly after platinum therapy). Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to an antibody‑drug conjugate bearing a topoisomerase I payload. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
* Active or progressive central nervous system metastasis or leptomeningeal disease; treated/stable brain metastases permissible under defined stability criteria.  
* Persistently greater than grade 1 toxicities from previous systemic anticancer therapy (except alopecia and vitiligo).  
* Recent major surgery, wide‑field radiotherapy, or focal palliation near target lesions exceeding stipulated intervals (excluded periods omitted per guidance).  
* Clinically significant untreated lung disease (interstitial pneumonitis, pulmonary fibrosis, severe radiation pneumonitis).  
* Current acute/chronic pancreatitis or cirrhosis not classified as Child‑Pugh A.  
* Recent (<60 days) hepatic decompensation manifestations (jaundice, encephalopathy, variceal bleeding).  
* History of liver transplantation or allogenic stem‑cell/bone‑marrow transplantation.  
* Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (recent MI, unstable angina, NYHA Class III–IV CHF, uncontrolled hypertension, serious arrhythmia, critical valve disease).  
* Thrombotic or cerebrovascular event within three months before enrolment.  
* Active uncontrolled infections—including bacterial, fungal, viral—and hepatitis B/C or HIV infection unless meeting predefined control parameters.  
* Allergy or hypersensitivity to the study agent or excipients.  
* Participation concurrently in another interventional clinical trial.  
* Pregnancy or breastfeeding.  
* Prior malignancy within three years apart from selected low‑risk exceptions (basal/squamous cell skin cancer, non‑muscle invasive bladder cancer, in‑situ cervix, breast, or prostate cancer with no activity for two years).  
* Any other serious acute or chronic medical, psychiatric, or laboratory condition judged by investigator to pose unacceptable risk or confound assessment.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >18 years. Sex allowed: all. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast carcinoma. Prior treatment excluded: more than two prior chemotherapy regimens in the metastatic setting; any prior megakaryocyte extracellular signal‑regulated kinase (MEK) inhibitor; prior discontinuation of abemaciclib for toxicity; prior discontinuation of fulvestrant for toxicity. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor positive or progesterone receptor positive and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of symptomatic congestive heart failure (NYHA Class II‑IV) or serious cardiac arrhythmia requiring treatment.; Myocardial infarction or unstable angina within six months preceding enrollment.; Clinically important resting ECG abnormalities (e.g., complete left bundle branch block, third‑degree AV block, prolonged PR > 250 ms) or QTcF >470 ms in men or women.; Inability or refusal to discontinue concomitant QT‑prolonging agents.; Current use of strong CYP3A4 inducers that cannot be stopped prior to study drug initiation.; Past or ongoing non‑infectious interstitial lung disease / pneumonitis or radiation‑induced pneumonitis requiring steroid therapy, or inability to rule out such pathology on screening scans.; Significant pulmonary comorbidity (recent pulmonary embolism, severe asthma, COPD, restrictive lung disease, substantial pleural effusion, autoimmune diseases with lung involvement, prior total pneumonectomy).; Active uncontrolled infection necessitating IV antimicrobial therapy.; Clinically significant corneal disease.; Known HIV infection.; Active viral hepatitis (B or C) outside stipulated low‑level/HBV suppressed or cured HCV parameters.; Pregnancy or nursing mothers (breastfeeding); Untreated or symptomatic brain metastases (requiring steroids or causing neurological deficits); Only asymptomatic, stable CNS metastases permissible under strict steroid limits.; Unresolved adverse events from previous anticancer therapy greater than CTCAE grade 1 (chronic grade 2 toxicities may be allowable case‑by‑case).; More than one primary malignancy diagnosed within three years (exemptions: fully excised non‑melanoma skin cancer, in‑situ carcinomas, appropriately treated superficial urothelial carcinoma, contralateral breast cancer).; Substance/alcohol dependence interfering with compliance.; Hypersensitivity to investigational product components.; Any condition judged by the investigator to render the participant unsafe or unlikely to comply with study requirements.

general_exclusion_criteria:

Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including symptomatic congestive heart failure, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, or serious arrhythmias.
Significant hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh class B/C) or bilirubin elevation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Cervical cancer. Histology allowed: Carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced inoperable, metastatic, or recurrent disease. Prior treatment required: Demonstrated target expression on ≥1 lesion by ^111In‑FPI‑1547 SPECT imaging AND disease refractory to standard systemic therapy or absence of applicable standard therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Nitrosoureas or actinomycin‑D anytime OR prior irradiation delivering >20 Gy to large areas of bone marrow. Biomarkers required: Availability of tumor tissue for IGF‑1R immunohistochemistry (to be assessed). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of severe pulmonary toxicities such as pneumonitis.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including symptomatic congestive heart failure, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, or serious arrhythmias.
Significant hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh class B/C) or bilirubin elevation.
Severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min).
Active central nervous system involvement not adequately controlled (uncontrolled brain metastases).
Known chronic infections such as untreated HIV, active Hepatitis B or C.
Performance status worse than ECOG 1.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of allergic reaction or hypersensitivity to investigational products, uncontrolled serious medical illnesses, concurrent use of prohibited anticancer therapies (except limited intrathecal chemotherapy, hydroxyurea, or a single low-dose cytarabine course), recent use of strong CYP3A inducers, active graft‑versus‑host disease requiring systemic therapy, severe gastrointestinal or metabolic disorders affecting drug absorption, ongoing treatment for another active malignant disease, baseline corrected QT interval >450 msec (unless discussed with investigator), documented active hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or human immunodeficiency virus infection, absolute neutrophil count exceeding protocol limits, inability/unwillingness to comply with mandated contraception measures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: endometrial cancer. Histology allowed: high‑grade endometrial carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease with radiographically verified progression per Response Evaluation Criteria In Solid Tumors version 1.1. Prior treatment required: prior platinum‑based chemotherapy **and** prior anti‑Programmed Death Receptor‑1 / Programmed Death Ligand‑1 immune checkpoint therapy. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to a cell‑cycle Checkpoint Kinase 1 (CHK1) inhibitor (including ACR‑368). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Known symptomatic brain metastases requiring greater than 10 mg daily prednisone (or equivalent).
Uncontrolled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection, Hepatitis B virus infection, or Hepatitis C virus infection.
Clinically meaningful coagulopathy or bleeding diathesis.
Significant cardiovascular disease, including uncontrolled hypertension (>160/90 mmHg), histories of torsades de pointes, substantial resting ECG abnormalities, pathological bradyarrhythmias, left bundle‑branch block, advanced atrioventricular blocks, sustained QTc prolongation (>450 ms in males or >470 ms in females), symptomatic congestive heart failure (NYHA Class III‑IV), unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction, severely reduced ejection fraction (<20%), or recent thromboembolic event (stroke/transient ischemic attack within six months).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: No radiographically evident local, regional, or distant metastasis (negative PSMA PET/Axumin scan). Prior treatment required: Radical prostatectomy (intra- or inter­facial) with documented successful bilateral nerve‐sparing and pathologic stage pN0 or pNX. Prior treatment excluded: Prior local prostate cancer therapy such as cryotherapy or brachytherapy; prior post‑prostatectomy or pelvic radiation therapy. Biomarkers required: PSA ≥0.1 ng/mL. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Known allergy to hyaluronic acid; pathologic T4 disease; prior local prostate cancer therapy (cryotherapy or brachytherapy); prior post‑prostatectomy or pelvic radiation therapy; planned elective pelvic nodal irradiation; prior extensive anorectal surgery resulting in loss of rectum; inflammatory bowel disease requiring steroid therapy; active connective tissue disorders (lupus, scleroderma); anatomical urogenital abnormalities precluding Barrigel injection; white blood cell count <4000/μL or >12,000/μL; hemoglobin <10 g/dL; active bleeding diathesis or coagulopathy (PTT >35 sec, INR >1.4, platelets <100,000/mm³); serum ALT/AST >2.5×ULN; serum creatinine >2.0 mg/dL; total bilirubin >2.0 mg/dL; chronic renal insufficiency; uncontrolled diabetes mellitus (fasting glucose >300 mg/dL); untreated/acquired immune deficiency syndrome (HIV/AIDS) without adequate CD4 control; contraindication to MRI or PSMA/PET scanning; bilateral hip prostheses incompatible with MRI; inability or unwillingness to follow study procedures; any condition deemed by investigators to interfere with safe administration or completion of study therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic castration‑resistant disease (≥1 metastatic lesion). Prior treatment required: androgen receptor pathway inhibitor **and** taxane‑based chemotherapy **and** prior lutetium‑177 labelled PSMA‑targeted radiopharmaceutical therapy with documented progression. Prior treatment excluded: Actinium‑225 based investigational compound. Biomarkers required: PSMA‑positive disease verified by PSMA PET/CT **and** castrate serum testosterone (<50 ng/dL). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of central nervous system metastases that are neurologically unstable, symptomatic, or require steroids for stability.; Current acute kidney injury occurring within 48 hours to 7 days or chronic kidney disease persisting >3 months.; Baseline xerostomia graded ≥2 by CTCAE version 5.; Uncontrolled hypertension, myocardial infarction, angina, or coronary artery bypass graft performed within 6 months preceding consent or any clinically active serious cardiovascular disease.; Presence of any other malignancy (excluding adequately treated basal cell carcinoma, actinic keratoses, or benign colorectal polyps) diagnosed within the last 5 years.; Poor performance status outside Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score 0‑2.; Significant hepatic impairment or bilirubin elevation exceeding protocol limits.; Active infection including untreated HIV or viral hepatitis.; Pregnancy or breastfeeding.; Inability to comply with study procedures or give informed consent.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≤30 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Wilms tumor. Histology allowed: Diffuse anaplastic. Cancer burden allowed: Stage II‑IV disease at initial diagnosis. Prior treatment required: No prior systemic therapy, except up to twelve (12) weeks of pre‑nephrectomy chemotherapy or up to six (6) weeks of postoperative chemotherapy as specifically permitted. Prior treatment excluded: Systemic therapy outside the allowed pre‑operative/post‑operative windows. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of bilateral Wilms tumor (synchronous or metachronous).
Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses including active infection or symptomatic congestive heart failure (grade ≥ 2).
Renal tubular acidosis (serum bicarbonate < 16 mmol/L and serum phosphate ≤ 2 mg/dL) for high‑risk or very‑high‑risk relapsed cohort.
Chronic inflammatory bowel disease and/or bowel obstruction for stage II‑IV diffuse anaplastic Wilm's tumor and standard‑risk relapsed cohort.
Concomitant use of St. John’s wort that cannot be discontinued.
Pregnancy or lactation in female participants of child‑bearing potential.
Failure to agree to effective contraception for sexually active individuals of reproductive potential.
History of significant pulmonary toxicity/pneumonitis.
Severe cardiac impairment (heart failure grades ≥ 2, EF < 40%).  
Significant renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance < 60 ml/min/1.73 m²) when not meeting laboratory exceptions.  
Hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin > 1.5× upper limit of normal, AST/ALT > 2.5× ULN).  
Active untreated HIV infection or viral hepatitis B/C.  
Uncontrolled central nervous system metastasis.

Prior treatment required: Received exactly two frontline chemotherapy agents (actinomycin D **AND** vincristine) resulting in documented remission before relapse. Prior treatment excluded: Any chemotherapy administered for the current relapse episode. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Clonal cytopenia of undetermined significance. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: High‑risk CCUS characterized by persistent unexplained cytopenia(s) for ≥4 months, laboratory-defined anemia (<12 g/dL women/<13 g/dL men) and/or neutropenia (<1.8×10⁹/L) and/or thrombocytopenia (<150×10⁹/L), and no morphological evidence of overt hematologic malignancy. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any cytotoxic chemotherapy, ionizing radiation therapy, immunomodulatory agents (e.g., lenalidomide), or targeted anti‑cancer therapies (e.g., PARP inhibitors) received within the prior 6 months or planned for the subsequent 6 months; concurrent primary malignancy requiring active cytotoxic chemotherapy and/or radiation therapy. Biomarkers required: Pathogenic variant in any myeloid driver gene with variant allele frequency ≥2% confirmed by next‑generation sequencing of peripheral blood or bone marrow (screened within 3 months of enrolment) and a high‑risk classification according to the Clonal Hematopoiesis Risk Score calculator. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of congenital/inherited bone marrow failure disorders or germline predispositions to hematologic malignancies.
Diagnosed myelodysplastic syndromes, myeloproliferative neoplasms, chronic myelomonocytic leukemia, acute myeloid leukaemia, or any other hematolymphoid malignancy ever diagnosed.
Concomitant hematologic precursor conditions such as smouldering multiple myeloma, smouldering Waldenström’s macroglobulinaemia, MGUS, or monoclonal B‑cell lymphocytosis.
Active uncontrolled systemic fungal, bacterial, or viral infection.
Recent (≤3 months) administration of live attenuated vaccines or planning to receive such vaccines within the next 15 months.
Evidence of clinically significant haemolysis.
Clinical hypersplenism or documented portal hypertension.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years and <85 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: high grade serous ovarian carcinoma **or** high grade endometrioid ovarian carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: recurring disease confined to the abdomen or pelvic region with measurable lesion(s) per RECIST 1.1 and **no** extra‑abdominal metastases. Prior treatment required: (at least one prior line of systemic therapy showing platinum‑resistant or intolerant disease **)** and (** prior poly(ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitor therapy as maintenance or treatment). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: BRCA1 mutation **or** BRCA2 mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of distant metastasis outside the abdomino‑pelvic cavity; recent (<3 months) bowel obstruction, high risk for obstruction, need for total parental nutrition or IV fluid dependency; concomitant investigation agent usage; solid organ transplantation; active second malignancy that is progressing or requires treatment, except certain low‑risk skin, cervical, bladder, prostate intraepithelial neoplasms or disease‑free >1 year cases; severe allergy or anaphylaxis to substances related to N‑803 or study products; prior immune‑related toxicity leading to permanent cessation of checkpoint inhibitors or requiring intense/prolonged immunosuppression (excluding controlled endocrine disorders); significant autoimmune disease (active IBD, rheumatologic, sclerosing, vasculitic, neuromuscular autoimmune conditions); chronic systemic glucocorticoids >10 mg prednisolone daily for ≥4 weeks before infusion; uncontrolled comorbidities—including active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious arrhythmia, uncontrolled hypertension, severe psychiatric disorder limiting protocol adherence; pregnancy or lactation; HIV positivity; untreated active hepatitis B or C; preexisting non‑infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; administration of live vaccination within 30 days preceding study treatment; documented anaphylactic reaction to murine‑based antibodies or iron dextran; history of grade 2 or greater hemolytic anemia.

Prior treatment required: Minimum one prior systemic therapy line; prior anthracycline exposure permitted provided cumulative dose ≤300 mg/m². general_exclusion_criteria: History of Grade ≥ 2 pneumonitis or pulmonary fibrosis. Uncontrolled congestive heart failure or symptomatic coronary artery disease. Left ventricular ejection fraction below institution’s lower limit of normal. Serum alanine aminotransferase or aspartate aminotransferase greater than three times the upper limit of normal. Bilirubin exceeding 1.5×upper limit of normal. Creatinine clearance less than 50 mL/min. Untreated or progressing central nervous system metastases. Known human immunodeficiency virus infection not controlled on antiretroviral therapy. Chronic hepatitis B or C infection not adequately suppressed. Pregnancy or lactation. Inability to comply with contraceptive requirements. Poor performance status (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score > 2). Ongoing participation in another interventional oncology trial. Severe ongoing acute medical illnesses requiring urgent intervention.

Bilirubin exceeding 1.5×upper limit of normal. Creatinine clearance less than 50 mL/min. Untreated or progressing central nervous system metastases. Known human immunodeficiency virus infection not controlled on antiretroviral therapy. Chronic hepatitis B or C infection not adequately suppressed. Pregnancy or lactation. Inability to comply with contraceptive requirements. Poor performance status (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score > 2). Ongoing participation in another interventional oncology trial. Severe ongoing acute medical illnesses requiring urgent intervention.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: metastatic breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable locally advanced or distant metastatic disease with measurable lesions per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: none mandatory beyond diagnosis; if hormone‐receptor‑positive tumour then prior CDK4/6 inhibitor and progression on or within 12 months of adjuvant endocrine therapy or at least one line of endocrine therapy in the metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: any prior topoisomerase I‑containing chemotherapy or antibody‑drug conjugate (except when specifically allowed for subsequent cohort), prior biologic or targeted therapy less than 14 days ago (ignored for timing), investigational anti‑cancer agents less than 21 days ago (timing ignored). Biomarkers required: HER2‑low (immunohistochemistry 1+ or 2+ with non‑amplified ISH) **or** HER2‑zero (Immunohistochemistry 0 regardless of ISH); estrogen‑receptor and progesterone‑receptor status any (% < 1% or ≥1%). Biomarkers excluded: HER2‑positive (IHC 3+ or ISH‑amplified).
general_exclusion_criteria: Concurrent use of other investigational antitumour agents.; Prior/topoisomerase I‑containing ADCs not permitted except per protocol.; Live attenuated vaccination within 30 days of enrolment.; Clinically significant corneal disease.; Severe hypersensitivity reaction to study drugs or other monoclonal antibodies.; Major surgery within 2 weeks prior to study entry.; Uncontrolled intercurrent illness, active infection, unmanaged non‑malignant systemic disease, uncontrolled seizures, or psychiatric/socioeconomic factors limiting compliance.; Current or unresolved pneumonitis /interstitial lung disease.; Significant preexisting pulmonary disorders (recent PE, severe asthma/COPD, restrictive disease, pleural effusions, autoimmune/connective‑tissue disease with lung involvement, prior pneumonectomy.). Prolonged corrected QT interval (>470 ms women, >450 ms men). Recent major cardiovascular events (CABG, PCI, MI, unstable angina, NYHA class II⁺ CHF, stroke) within six months.; Second malignant neoplasm unless disease free ≥3 years or meets listed exceptions (in‑situ cervix/prostate, superficial bladder, non‑melanoma skin cancer). ; Untreated or poorly controlled HIV infection.; Active or untreated Hepatitis B or C infection.; Active infectious processes including tuberculosis.; Pregnancy, lactation, or plans to conceive during study period.; Conditions, therapies, or lab abnormalities that could confound outcomes or impede safe completion of the trial.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Renal cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma (sarcomatoid variants accepted). Cancer burden allowed: Advanced or metastatic disease with measurable lesion(s). Prior treatment required: At least one immune checkpoint inhibitor and at least one tyrosine kinase inhibitor (given in neoadjuvant, adjuvant, or metastatic setting). Prior treatment excluded: Any prior PSMA‑targeted therapy (e.g., ^177Lu‑PSMA‑617). Biomarkers required: Positive PSMA uptake on ^68Ga‑PSMA‑PET/CT (lesion intensity greater than liver) and absence of PSMA‑negative lesions larger than 1 cm. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Untreated brain metastases.  
Active/leptomeningeal central nervous system involvement.  
Serious uncontrolled medical conditions (e.g., interstitial lung disease, severe dyspnea requiring supplemental O₂, severe renal impairment [eGFR < 30 mL/min/1.73 m²], inflammatory bowel disease with baseline grade ≥ 2 diarrhea).  
Allergic reaction to components chemically related to ^177Lu‑PSMA‑617.  
Symptomatic or imminent spinal cord compression.  
Concurrent active malignancies expected to interfere with study assessments (except certain low-risk cured tumors).  
Pregnant or nursing individuals.  
Performance status worse than Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score ≤ 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate adenocarcinoma. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: NA. general_exclusion_criteria: History of major adverse cardiac event (myocardial infarction, new/congestive heart failure, or stroke) within the past six months; Uncontrolled intercurrent illness; Inability to swallow oral medication; Known severe hypersensitivity to leuprolide depot or relugolix or structurally similar substances.

general_exclusion_criteria: Active hepatitis B virus infection, active hepatitis C virus infection, positive human immunodeficiency virus serology (unless predefined exceptions are met), receipt of any major surgical procedure within 28 days preceding first dose, inability to comply with protocol requirements, uncontrolled autoimmune haemolytic anaemia or immune thrombocytopaenia requiring treatment.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Adenoid cystic carcinoma of the salivary glands (head and neck). general_exclusion_criteria:
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness (e.g., active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmia).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Uncorrectable bleeding or coagulation disorder increasing procedural risk.
Severe hepatic impairment, renal insufficiency, or hematologic abnormalities contraindicating safe participation.
Significant neurologic conditions (unmanaged seizures, untreated central nervous system lesions) compromising protocol compliance.
Active secondary malignancy requiring concurrent therapy.
Psychiatric or social circumstances impairing ability to give informed consent or comply with study procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor (any primary site). Histology allowed: any malignant histology of solid tumors. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of spinal cord compression.
Active leptomeningeal disease or uncontrolled, untreated brain metastasis.
Any additional malignancy diagnosed within 2 years preceding enrollment, excluding certain localized skin cancers, superficial bladder cancer, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, or carcinoma in situ of the breast when cured with curative intent.
History of interstitial lung disease, grade 2 or higher non‑infectious pneumonitis, resting arterial oxygen saturation less than 92 %, or requirement for supplemental oxygen at baseline.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus.
Grade greater than 1 persistent abnormalities in serum potassium, sodium, or corrected calcium despite appropriate medical management.
Systemic bacterial, fungal, or viral infection requiring antimicrobial therapy within 14 days prior to dosing.

Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All genders. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Presence of at least one active and measurable intracranial metastasis (size ≥10 mm by CT/MRI) with no leptomeningeal disease. Neurological symptoms must be stable ≥2 weeks. Prior treatment required: At least one endocrine therapy administered in the metastatic setting; documented radiographic progression on or after the most recent systemic therapy; ECOG performance status ≤2. Prior treatment excluded: More than two prior chemotherapy regimens; more than two prior cyclin‐dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor regimens (any agent except abemaciclib); prior exposure to abemaciclib in the metastatic setting; prior exposure to elacestrant or any investigational selective estrogen receptor degrader/modulator/conjugate/proteolysis targeting chimera in the metastatic setting. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive (≥1 % cells stained by IHC) **and** Human epidermal growth factor receptor‑2 negative (IHC 0‑1+ or ISH negative). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Immediate central nervous system‑specific treatment anticipated; imminent organ failure or visceral crisis; leptomeningeal carcinomatosis; lack of prior systemic therapy in the advanced/metastatic setting; prior receipt of abemaciclib in the metastatic setting (adjuvant use acceptable if >12 months before relapse); prior receipt of elacestrant or similar investigational hormonal agents in the metastatic setting; concurrent malignant neoplasm requiring active treatment within past three years (exceptions noted); enrolment in another therapeutic breast cancer trial (observational studies permissible after washout); untreated/uncontrolled active infections including unresolved HBV, HCV, or HIV viremia; uncontrolled symptomatic brain metastases; major surgical procedure within four weeks preceding study entry; inability to ingest oral medication or conditions causing malabsorption; pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraception for females of reproductive potential and their male partners; known hypersensitivity to study drugs or formulation components; concomitant use of strong/moderate CYP3A4 modulators; intake of restricted herbal products shortly before dosing; recent vaccinations (within seven days); any severe medical or psychiatric disorder judged by investigators to compromise safety or compliance.

general_exclusion_criteria:
extensive leptomeningeal disease  
QTc > 450 ms in men or > 470 ms in women  
history of another malignancy within the previous 2 years (exceptions permitted for in‑situ lesions or basal/squamous cell skin cancer)

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma. Histology allowed: WHO grade IV glioblastoma, inclusive of gliosarcoma. Biomarkers required: IDH wild‑type (determined by immunohistochemistry or DNA sequencing). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

extensive leptomeningeal disease

Prior treatment required: Completed external‐beam radiation therapy for head and neck cancer ≥3 years prior to screening. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: Active Epstein–Barr virus infection (positive anti‑VCA IgM), active hepatitis C virus infection, Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of systemic autoimmune disease involving the salivary glands (e.g., Sjögren’s syndrome).
Use of systemic immunosuppressive agents within 4 weeks of enrollment (topical/inhaled/nasal steroids permissible).
Diagnosis of myasthenia gravis.
Personal or familial predisposition to acute/chronic angle‑closure glaucoma or prior prophylaxis for angle‑closure glaucoma.
Known allergy or hypersensitivity to glycopyrrolate.
Current cigarette smoking or former smoking/vaping with nicotine/tobacco products within the preceding 3 years.
Current excessive alcohol consumption or similar pattern within the preceding 3 years (men >14 drinks/week, women >7 drinks/week in North America; >14 units/week for both genders in the United Kingdom).
Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus (Hemoglobin A1c > 7%).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: endometrial cancer. Histology allowed: any. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent disease with measurable lesions per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: at least one prior systemic chemotherapy regimen for endometrial carcinoma **and** prior immunotherapy targeting the PD‑1/PD‑L1 pathway (administered in adjuvant, first‑line, or recurrent settings). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: ARID1A loss‑of‑function mutation identified by a CLIA‑certified next‑generation sequencing assay (assessment performed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Recent chemotherapy or radiotherapy within 4 weeks (6 weeks for nitrosoureas or mitomycin C); unresolved adverse events greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy unless judged stable; simultaneous participation in another interventional investigational study; untreated or progressive central nervous system metastases (meningeal carcinomatosis excluded); severe hypersensitivity reaction to avelumab, M1774, or similar biologics; active uncontrolled infection (exceptions made for well‑managed HIV, HBV, HCV under stipulated conditions); major cardiovascular disease—including symptomatic congestive heart failure NYHA class III‑IV, uncontrolled hypertension, serious arrhythmias, recent myocardial infarction, stroke, or cerebrovascular accident within 180 days; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression (mild endocrinopathies permissible); history of solid-organ or hematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation; recent severe gastrointestinal pathology such as bowel obstruction, uncontrolled diarrhea, or ongoing inflammatory bowel disease; significant psychiatric disorder or recent suicide attempt affecting ability to comply; pregnancy or breastfeeding, or insufficient contraception in women of child‑bearing potential; use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 or 1A2 enzyme modifiers, proton‑pump inhibitors, or other prohibited concomitant medicines within 7 days; live vaccinations within 4 weeks of initiating study treatment (inactive vaccines allowed); consumption of herbal supplements or folk remedies (e.g., St. John’s wort, kava, ephedra, Ginkgo biloba, DHEA, yohimbine, saw palmetto, ginseng).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder, renal pelvis, ureter or urethra. general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis or active pulmonary fibrotic disorders; active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; significant cardiovascular disease including recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, severe arrhythmias, NYHA class III or higher heart failure, or left ventricular ejection fraction < 40%; uncontrolled infectious diseases; hepatic impairment exceeding stipulated laboratory limits; renal impairment with glomerular filtration rate < 30 ml/min/1.73 m²; uncontrolled diabetes (HbA₁c > 8%); ongoing high-dose glucocorticoid or immunosuppressive therapy; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment; unresolved grade ≥ 2 toxicities from prior anticancer therapy; concomitant enrollment in another interventional oncology trial; inability to comply with study assessments due to psychiatric or social circumstances.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis.
Uncontrolled cardiac insufficiency or congestive heart failure.
Significant hepatic impairment.
Severe renal dysfunction.
Active untreated/uncontrolled central nervous system metastasis.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection or chronic viral hepatitis.
Performance status poorer than required (e.g., Karnofsky/Lansky score < 50%). 
Pregnancy or breastfeeding (nursing mothers). 
Concurrent second malignant neoplasm (except secondary brain tumour in remission ≥ 5 yrs). 
Clinically significant gastrointestinal diseases affecting absorption (severe dysphagia, malabsorptive syndromes, inflammatory bowel disease, extensive gastrectomy/resections). 
Any severe unrelated systemic illness compromising safety. 
Corrected QT interval greater than 480 milliseconds. 
Use of other anti‑cancer or investigational therapeutic agents concurrently. 
Concomitant anticoagulation with warfarin or lipid‑lowering therapy with statins.

Hypersensitivity to durvalumab, trastuzumab deruxtecan, or their components.  
Major surgery within 4 weeks preceding study treatment.  
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness—including active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia, severe chronic gastrointestinal disease causing diarrhea, or psychiatric/social circumstances limiting compliance.  
Chronic systemic glucocorticoids >10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent or other systemic immunosuppressive therapy within the past 2 years.  
Active or prior autoimmune/inflammatory disorders requiring systemic therapy (exceptions: vitiligo, alopecia, stable hypothyroidism, mild chronic skin conditions, well‑controlled celiac disease, inactive disease >5 years).  
History of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, or currently unresolved ILD/pneumonitis.  
Pulmonary co‑morbidities such as recent pulmonary embolism, severe asthma, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, restrictive lung disease, substantial pleural effusion, or rheumatologic diseases with pulmonary involvement; prior pneumonectomy.  
Corrected QT interval >470 ms or any condition/drug that increases risk of QT prolongation/torsade de pointe.  
Cardiac events or procedures within 6 months: coronary artery bypass graft, angioplasty, vascular stenting, myocardial infarction, angina, New York Heart Association Class II or greater heart failure, or cerebrovascular accident/stroke; elevated troponin without obvious acute MI necessitating cardiology consult.  
Left ventricular ejection fraction <50%.  
Other malignant histories except adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or solid tumors eradicated with curative intent ≥5 years ago.  
Venous thromboembolic event within the past 3 months.  
Primary immunodeficiency.  
Active infectious diseases: untreated tuberculosis, active hepatitis B (surface antigen positive), active hepatitis C (RNA positive), or HIV infection.  
Receipt of a live attenuated vaccine within 30 days prior to dosing.  
Concomitant use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4, 2C9, or 2D6 inhibitors/inducers/substrates violating protocol restrictions.  
Hydroxychloroquine taken within 14 days prior to day 1 of trastuzumab deruxtecan.  
Leptomeningeal carcinomatosis.  
Female participants who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or of reproductive potential unwilling to use mandated contraception throughout study treatment and appropriate postpartum intervals; similarly, male participants with sexual activity involving women of childbearing potential who decline prescribed contraception protocols.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18-59 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: acute myeloid leukemia (all morphological subtypes except acute promyelocytic leukemia). Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed, untreated disease meeting high‑risk/adverse classification per European LeukemiaNet 2017 criteria (including therapy‑related AML, AML evolving from an antecedent hematologic disorder, and AML with myelodysplasia‑related changes). Prior treatment required: none (patient must be untreated for AML; however hydroxyurea for leukocytosis control, a single dose of intrathecal chemotherapy, brief all‑trans retinoic acid administered until acute promyelocytic leukemia is ruled out, prior anthracycline exposure not exceeding a cumulative lifetime dose of 200 mg/m² daurinobucin or equivalent, and prior hypomethylating-agent exposure for conditions other than AML are permissible). Prior treatment excluded: any AML‑directed induction, consolidation, or maintenance therapy; receipt of investigational anticancer agents; simultaneous use of other therapeutic agents targeting AML. Biomarkers required: absent FLT3 internal tandem duplication and absent FLT3 tyrosine‑kinase domain mutation **and** absent t(9;22)(Philadelphia chromosome) translocation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of Wilson’s disease or other copper‑metabolism disorder.
Pregnancy or lactation; unwillingness or inability to employ approved dual contraception methods.
Concurrent malignancy requiring active therapy (hormonal therapy exempt).
Receipt of or plan to receive additional investigational agents during study participation.
Performance status Zubrod ≥ 3.
Left ventricular ejection fraction less than 50%.
Aspartate aminotransferase or alanine aminotransferase greater than three times the upper limit of normal, or total bilirubin greater than twice the upper limit of normal (unless historical Gilberts syndrome permits higher level).
Creatinine clearance lower than 30 ml/min.

general_exclusion_criteria: Untreated or symptomatic central nervous system metastases, or lesions requiring radiation, surgery, or ongoing steroid therapy.; Leptomeningeal disease.; Concurrent systemic anticancer therapy.; Major surgery or radiotherapy performed within three weeks preceding study treatment.; Persistent greater-than-grade‑1 clinically significant toxicities attributed to prior anticancer therapies.; History of hypersensitivity reactions to any excipient of the investigational product.; Unstable angina, congestive heart failure classified as New York Heart Association class II‑IV, serious cardiac arrhythmia requiring treatment, or myocardial infarction occurring within six months.; Presence of another current malignancy except fully resected cervical intra­ epithelial neoplasia, non‑melanoma skin cancer, or other curatively treated low‑risk cancer without evidence of disease.; Need for continuous home oxygen therapy due to resting dyspnoea of any cause.; Serious acute or chronic medical conditions including uncontrolled infection, significant pulmonary, metabolic, or psychiatric disorder.; Documented infectious diseases as specified in the protocol.; Pregnancy or breastfeeding.; Primary tumour arising in the nasopharynx irrespective of histology.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of autoimmune disease or immune deficiency; prior idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, idiopathic pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis on baseline imaging; active tuberculosis; serious infections within four weeks; receipt of therapeutic antibiotics within two weeks (except prophylaxis); clinically significant cardiovascular disease; administration of live attenuated vaccine within four weeks or anticipated need during study; prior allogenic bone marrow or solid organ transplantation; recent systemic immunostimulatory agents (within four weeks or five half‑lives); recent systemic immunosuppressive drugs (aside from permitted low‑dose steroids for COPD/asthma or adrenal insufficiency); unresolved central nervous system disease, uncontrolled seizure disorder, recent (< six months) stroke, transient ischemic attack, or subarachnoid haemorrhage.

Active autoimmune disease needing systemic immunosuppression.
Untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.
Clinically significant infectious diseases (active bacterial, fungal, viral infection requiring therapy).
HIV-positive or active Hepatitis B or C infection.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Inadequate bone marrow, hepatic, or renal function outside predefined lab limits.

general_exclusion_criteria: Serious medical, laboratory, or psychiatric condition prohibiting signed informed consent; Pregnancy, lactation, or intent to become pregnant during study; Central nervous system involvement by Waldenström macroglobulinemia; Documented human immunodeficiency virus infection; Active hepatitis B virus infection (positive HBsAg or HBV DNA) or active hepatitis C virus infection (detectable HCV RNA); Concurrent systemic immunosuppressants exceeding 20 mg prednisone daily equivalence; Active or ongoing autoimmune hemolytic anemia or immune thrombocytopenia; Co-administration of warfarin or related agents; Live attenuated vaccinations administered within four weeks prior to first study dose; Active uncontrolled bacterial, viral, fungal, or parasitic infection (aside from superficial fungal nail infection); Major surgery occurring within four weeks before initiating study drug; History of severe bleeding disorders, hemophilia, or spontaneous bleeds requiring transfusion; Stroke or intracranial hemorrhage within six months before first dose; Inability to swallow oral tablets; Anticipated non‑compliance with outpatient treatment, labs, or scheduled visits; Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease—including unstable angina within three months, myocardial infarction within six months, left ventricular ejection fraction ≤45 % within twelve months, NYHA class III–IV heart failure, or life‑ threatening arrhythmias; Known hypersensitivity to any excipient of zanubrutinib, rituximab, or bendamustine; Prior malignancy within the past three years excluding adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin cancer, non‑muscle invasive bladder cancer, cervical or breast carcinoma in situ, or localized Gleason grade 6 prostate cancer; Severe or disabling pulmonary disease; Ongoing alcohol or illicit drug dependency; Psychiatric conditions impairing adherence to study protocols; Use of strong cytochrome P450 3A enzyme inducers.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 to 65 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. general_exclusion_criteria:
Unresolved non‑hematologic toxicities greater than Grade 1 (except alopecia).
Receipt of gemtuzumab ozogamicin (Mylotarg) or other agents linked to heightened risk of sinusoidal obstruction syndrome/VOD, or existing/past VOD.
Active extracranial central nervous system, ocular, or testicular disease (immune‑privileged sites).
Significant cardiac events including myocardial infarction within the preceding 2 years.
Venous thromboembolism (deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolus) within the preceding 12 months (unless resolved and off anticoagulation).
Stroke or transient ischemic attack within the preceding 12 months.
Recent (≤6 months) clinically significant gastrointestinal hemorrhage, peptic ulcer disease, erosive gastritis, bowel perforation, or massive hemoptysis.
History of thrombotic microangiopathies such as atypical haemolytic uraemic syndrome or thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura.
Severe hypersensitivity to intravenous iron preparations or murine-derived monoclonal antibodies.
Presence of clinically significant donor‑specific anti‑human leucocyte antigen antibodies necessitating desensitization procedures.
Incapacity to discontinue concomitant medicines that strongly inhibit or induce cytochrome P450 pathways affecting cyclophosphamide or thiotepa metabolism during the designated peri‑transplant window.
Ongoing untreated bacterial, viral, or invasive fungal infection.
Previous receipt of any allogeneic or autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplantation, or any solid‑organ transplantation.
Medical conditions contraindicating delivery of myeloablative total‑body irradiation (e.g., prior excessive radiation exposure, inherited DNA repair disorders such as Fanconi anemia).
Human immunodeficiency virus infection with ongoing antiretroviral therapy.
Serological evidence of chronic hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection.
Pregnancy or lactation; lack of effective contraception for women of child‑bearing potential and males throughout the study and for stipulated post‑therapy intervals.
Psychiatric disorder or social circumstance judged insufficient to assure adherence to study protocols.
Any additional active non‑haematologic malignancy unless disease free for ≥5 years (with the sole exceptions of cervica­l intraepithelial neoplasia, basal cell carcinoma, or cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma diagnosed/treated within the last five years).

Receipt of gemtuzumab ozogamicin (Mylotarg) or other agents linked to heightened risk of sinusoidal obstruction syndrome/VOD, or existing/past VOD.
Active extracranial central nervous system, ocular, or testicular disease (immune‑privileged sites).
Significant cardiac events including myocardial infarction within the preceding 2 years.
Venous thromboembolism (deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolus) within the preceding 12 months (unless resolved and off anticoagulation).
Stroke or transient ischemic attack within the preceding 12 months.
Recent (≤6 months) clinically significant gastrointestinal hemorrhage, peptic ulcer disease, erosive gastritis, bowel perforation, or massive hemoptysis.
History of thrombotic microangiopathies such as atypical haemolytic uraemic syndrome or thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura.
Severe hypersensitivity to intravenous iron preparations or murine-derived monoclonal antibodies.
Presence of clinically significant donor‑specific anti‑human leucocyte antigen antibodies necessitating desensitization procedures.
Incapacity to discontinue concomitant medicines that strongly inhibit or induce cytochrome P450 pathways affecting cyclophosphamide or thiotepa metabolism during the designated peri‑transplant window.
Ongoing untreated bacterial, viral, or invasive fungal infection.
Previous receipt of any allogeneic or autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplantation, or any solid‑organ transplantation.
Medical conditions contraindicating delivery of myeloablative total‑body irradiation (e.g., prior excessive radiation exposure, inherited DNA repair disorders such as Fanconi anemia).
Human immunodeficiency virus infection with ongoing antiretroviral therapy.
Serological evidence of chronic hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection.
Pregnancy or lactation; lack of effective contraception for women of child‑bearing potential and males throughout the study and for stipulated post‑therapy intervals.
Psychiatric disorder or social circumstance judged insufficient to assure adherence to study protocols.
Any additional active non‑haematologic malignancy unless disease free for ≥5 years (with the sole exceptions of cervica­l intraepithelial neoplasia, basal cell carcinoma, or cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma diagnosed/treated within the last five years).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor. Histology allowed: any solid malignant histology. general_exclusion_criteria:
Active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.
Unresolved adverse events from prior therapy exceeding CTCAE version 5.0 grade 1.
Significant cardiovascular disease including unstable angina, recent acute coronary syndrome, history of myocardial infarction, or documented reduced left ventricular ejection fraction.
Active uncontrolled systemic bacterial, viral, fungal, or parasitic infection.
Known active hepatitis B virus infection or hepatitis C virus infection.
Presence of another concurrent active malignancy unless in remission with anticipated survival longer than two years.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive carcinoma (any subtype). Cancer burden allowed: locoregional advanced or metastatic disease; at least one measurable extracranial lesion per RECIST v1.1 not confined to a previously radiated field; bone‐only disease not sufficient; prior treated and stable central nervous system metastases permissible. Prior treatment required: 1–3 prior chemotherapy regimens for metastatic breast cancer, with at least one regimen containing an antibody‑drug conjugate; must not have progressed on prior platinum chemotherapy in the metastatic setting (platinum exposure in adjuvant/neoadjuvant settings allowed if relapse occurred >6 months after completing platinum). Prior treatment excluded: documented progression on prior platinum chemotherapy in the metastatic setting; any prior Wee1 kinase inhibitor therapy. Biomarkers required: Estrogen‑receptor negative (≤10 % nuclear stain by IHC) **and** Progesterone‑receiver negative (≤10 % nuclear stain by IHC) **and** Human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (score 0 or 1⁺ by IHC **or** ISH ratio < 2.0). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Major surgical procedure within 28 days of registration; uncontrolled intercurrent illness (e.g., active infection, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, NYHA class III/IV heart failure, recent MI, uncontrolled diabetes, serious GI ulcers, hepatic or renal insufficiency, severe malnutrition); clinically significant (>grade 1) lingering toxicities from prior therapy (neuropathy, other grade ≥2 toxicities except alopecia/anorexia); history of allergic reaction or severe hypersensitivity (≥grade 3) to az­enosertib, carboplat​inum, pembrolizumab or related compounds; prior/non‑infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease requiring steroids or presently active; inability to tolerate oral intake (presence of PEG tube or dependence on total parental nutrition); receipt of any investigational product or device within 4 weeks preceding enrolment; live or attenuated vaccination within 30 days before first dose; uncontrolled central nervous system disease (untreated/symptomatic brain metastases or recent cranial surgery/resection within 1 month); prolonged QTc > 470 ms or predisposition to torsades de pointes, concomitant QT‑prolonging meds; active tuberculosis or other active infection requiring systemic therapy; active autoimmune disorder necessitating systemic therapy within past 2 years (exempt hormonal replacements); immunodeficiency or systemic glucocorticoid/immunosuppressive therapy >10 mg prednisolone equivalents within 7 days; uncontrolled psychiatric or substance‑use disorder impairing compliance; pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant/father a child during study window and up to stipulated postpartum periods; solid organ or stem‑cell transplantation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Birth to 21 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Low grade glioma. Histology allowed: Low‑grade glial/neuroglial tumor. Participant’s tumor has additional previously‑known activating molecular alterations.
Participant exhibits symptoms without radiographically evident recurrent or progressive disease.
Known or suspected neurofibromatosis type 1 diagnosed by genetic testing or established clinical criteria.

Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic or locoregional therapy for prostate cancer—including androgen deprivation therapy lasting more than 30 days, anti‑androgens, abiraterone, apalutamide, prednis(???)tONE, chemotherapy, radiation therapy, prostate surgery, brachytherapy, or receipt of exogenous androgens, estrogens, or progestogens within six months. Prior LHRHa usage >30 days excluded. Biomarkers required: Informed consent for tumor genomic sequencing and provision of adequate prostate tumor tissue for next‑generation sequencing. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Stage T4 prostate cancer by clinical exam or imaging.
Hypogonadism or severe androgen deficiency (testosterone >50 ng/dL below institutional lower limit).
Active infection or chronic liver disease requiring systemic therapy.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection with detectable viral load.
Uncontrolled or recently significant cardiovascular disease (recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, symptomatic pericarditis, recent coronary interventions, NYHA class III–IV heart failure, recent major arrhythmias).
Uncontrolled hypertension.
Inability to swallow oral medications or gastrointestinal disorders affecting drug absorption (ulcerative colitis, Crohn’s disease, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting/diarrhea, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small bowel resections).
History of hypersensitivity to study drugs or excipients.
Documented non‑compliance with medical regimens.
Severe concurrent illness, infection, or comorbidity rendering participation unsafe or unsuitable for radical prostatectomy.
Receipt of any androgen deprivation therapy, chemotherapy, prostate surgery, or radiation therapy prior to enrollment.
Concurrent administration of androgens, estrogens, or progesteronal agents, or exposure to these agents within six months preceding randomization.
Evidence of distant metastasis (M1) on conventional imaging modalities such as computed tomography or technetium‑99m bone scintigraphy.

major surgery (other than surgery for recurrent diffuse glioma) within four weeks preceding enrollment; receipt of conventional external beam radiotherapy within the preceding twelve weeks unless progression occurs outside the irradiated volume or is histologically verified; prior cranial radiotherapy exceeding 65 Gray, stereotactic radiosurgery, or brachytherapy unless recurrence is histologically proved; ongoing full‑dose anticoagulation without therapeutic coagulation parameters stabilized for at least two weeks; concomitant use of strong CYP2D6/CYP2C19 substrate medicines that cannot be substituted; use of enzyme‑inducing antiepileptic drugs (patients must switch to non‑enzyme‑inducing alternatives at least two weeks before enrollment); significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral drug absorption (ulcers, inflammatory bowel disease, short‑gut syndrome, etc.); cardiovascular events within six months (myocardial infarction, unstable angina, coronary or peripheral arterial bypass, symptomatic congestive heart failure, stroke or transient ischemic attack); prior invasive malignant neoplasm (aside from adequately treated basal/squamous/non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or localized tumors disease‑free ≥3 years); and any other severe acute or chronic medical, psychiatric, or laboratory abnormalities judged by investigator to pose unacceptable risk or confound outcome assessments.)

Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses—including active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction, cerebrovascular accident, or serious cardiac arrhythmias. Presence of another concurrently advancing malignancy (exceptions: adequately treated cutaneous basal/squamous cell carcinoma, in‑situ cervical cancer). Positive serologic tests for hepatitis B or hepatitis C viruses (unless cleared). Pregnant or nursing individuals, or persons unwilling to employ effective contraception throughout the study period.

Biomarkers required: Confirmatory demonstration of IDH wildtype status; presence of EGFR amplification **or** TERT promoter mutation **or** simultaneous chromosome 7 gain and chromosome 10 loss (any one suffices). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of active infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy; Positive serology for HIV, hepatitis B surface antigen, or hepatitis C antibody; Known active tuberculosis; Past or current myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukaemia; Autoimmune disease necessitating systemic immunomodulatory therapy within the preceding 2 years; Ongoing systemic steroid usage exceeding dexamethasone >2 mg/day equivalent for ≥3 continuous days within 2 weeks of study start; Recent (<6 months) myocardial infarction, symptomatic unstable angina, New York Heart Association class III–IV congestive heart failure, serious ventricular or supraventricular arrhythmias, conduction disorder requiring pacing, moderate/severe valve disease, symptomatic pericarditis, or uncontrolled ischemia; Baseline corrected QT interval >500 ms; Significant electrolytes derangements; Clinically meaningful hepatic dysfunction (AST/ALT >2.5 times upper normal limits, bilirubin >1.5 times UNL) or renal dysfunction (serum creatinine >1.5 times UNL or CrCl <50 ml/min); Gastrointestinal conditions impairing drug absorption (severe diarrhoea grade ≥2, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small-bowel resection); Major surgery within 4 weeks prior to initiation; History of clinically significant haemorrhage or coagulation disorder increasing bleeding risk; Receipt of live vaccinations within 30 days before study entry; Substance/alcohol dependence interfering with adherence; Concurrent participation in another interventional oncology trial.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Renal cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: All renal cell carcinoma histologies (clear cell, papillary, chromophobes, sarcomatoid, collecting duct, etc.). Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable locoregional disease (clinical stage TxN1Mx) or metastatic disease (TxNxM1) with measurable extracranial disease per RECIST 1.1; International Metastatic Database Consortium intermediate risk (1–2 adverse factors) or poor risk (≥3 adverse factors). Prior treatment required: Initiation of a standard‑of‑care immunotherapy‑based combination regimen within 90 days prior to registration comprising either a programmed death‑1/programmed death ligand‑1 inhibitor together with a cytotoxic T‑lymphocyte associated antigen‑4 inhibitor (example: nivolumab + ipilimumab) **or** a programmed death‑1/programmed death ligand‑1 inhibitor together with a vascular endothelial growth factor pathway targeted agent (examples: nivolumab + axitinib, pembrolizumab + axitinib, atezolizumab + bevacizumab). Prior treatment excluded: Prior systemic therapy for metastatic renal cell carcinoma initiated greater than 90 days before registration; prior radiotherapy to the affected kidney that would overlap proposed SABR fields; definitive curative intent treatment (surgical resection, ablative radiation, or interventional procedure) directed at all metastatic sites; untreated or unstable central nervous system metastasis; any systemic anticancer therapy not belonging to the specified immunotherapy‑based combinations. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis, active or unresolved infectious diseases (active tuberculosis, uncontrolled HIV/HBV/HCV infections), congestive heart failure classified as New York Heart Association Class III‑IV, uncontrolled hypertension (systolic ≥190 mmHg or diastolic >110 mmHg), clinically significant cardiovascular event (myocardial infarction, stroke, transient ischemic attack) within 180 days, symptomatic peripheral artery disease, recent major surgery or invasive procedures within 28 days, grade ≥3 acute toxicities from prior therapy, Grade ≥2 bleeding disorders, platelet count <50,000/mm³, hemoglobin <8 g/dL despite transfusion support, absolute neutrophil count <1500/mm³, serum creatinine clearance <30 ml/min, total bilirubin >1.5×upper limit of normal (unless Gilberts syndrome), AST/ALT >3×(or >5× with hepatic metastases) upper limit of normal, active autoimmune disease requiring systemic glucocorticoids >10 mg/day prednisolone equivalent, uncontrolled diabetes mellitus, psychiatric illness impairing compliance, pregnancy or lactation, inability to comply with contraceptive requirements.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of prior or concurrent malignancy that could confound assessment of study outcomes.
Active central nervous system metastases.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness, including predefined cardiac diseases.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor (metastatic or unresectable). Histology allowed: Malignancies of epithelial, mesenchymal, neuroectodermal origin (all solid tumor histologies). Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease or locally unresectable disease; patients with treated/stable brain metastasis are allowed, and patients with asymptomatic active brain metastasis are allowed if no urgent CNS intervention anticipated in Cycle 1. Prior treatment required: Receipt of a poly(ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitor if the patient’s cancer type has an FDA‑approved PARP inhibitor (otherwise patient may be PARP‑inhibitor‑naïve or have PARP‑inhibitor‑resistant disease). Prior treatment excluded: None (aside from prohibited investigational agents, see boilerplate). Biomarkers required: Documented pathogenic mutation (germline or somatic) in any of the following DNA‑damage‑repair genes—as determined by a CLIA‑ certified assay: BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, RAD51C, RAD51D, ATM, BARD1, BLM, BRIP1, CDK12, FANCA, FANCC, FANCD2, FANCE, FANCF, FANCM, MRE11A, NBN, RAD50, RAD51B. Biomarkers excluded: Homozygous UGT1A1 *28 (TA)_7_TAA_, homozygous UGT1A1 *6 (G71R), or compound heterozygote (*28 plus *6) alleles.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Recent cytotoxic chemotherapy or radiation therapy within protocol-specified windows (excluded as washout; therefore not listed in spaces).
• Residual toxicity > Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia).
• Concurrent use of other investigational agents.
• Known hypersensitivity to novobiocin or chemically related compounds.
• Concomitant drugs that strongly inhibit or induce CYP3A4/5, are substrates of BCRP/ABC​G2, OATP1B1/1B3/2B1, or OAT1/3 administered ≤ 14 days before study drug.
• Herbal or dietary supplements with recognized hepatotoxic risk taken ≤ 14 days before study drug.
• Medications posing significant QTc prolongation/TdP risk started ≤ 14 days before study drug (with exceptions noted for granisetron and limited ondansetron use).
• Uncontrolled intercurrent illness, acute or severely compromised liver disease/cirrhosis, or inadequate renal function (GFR < 60 mL/min).
• Active or untreated infections requiring systemic therapy (excluding well‑controlled HIV/HBV/HCV as detailed in inclusion).
• Significant cardiovascular disease (NYHA Class III/IV, unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction, uncontrolled hypertension).
• Poor performance status (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group ≥ 3 / Karnofsky ≤ 50%).  
• Pregnancy or lactation; women of childbearing potential and males must use highly effective contraception throughout study and for four months post‑treatment.  
• Inability to give informed consent or lack of legal authority to consent.

Unresolved/interfering toxicity from prior anticancer therapy greater than grade 1 (aside from alopecia).
Significant cardiovascular disease (recent MI, NYHA Class II‑IV CHF, unstable angina, serious arrhythmias, LVEF < 50%, prolonged QT/QTc intervals).
Baseline interstitial lung disease, pulmonary fibrosis, or active pneumonitis.

Concurrent systemic steroids >10 mg prednisone daily or other immunosuppressive therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: Either squamous or non‑squamous NSCLC. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced or metastatic disease not amenable to curative surgery or radiation. Prior treatment required: Evidence of KRAS‑G12C mutation identified in tumor tissue or circulating DNA; receipt of at least two prior systemic regimens for locally advanced or metastatic disease, including one KRAS‑G12C‑targeted therapy (may count as two if combined with immunotherapy); radiographic disease progression on or after the most recent therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Targeted therapy directed at any actionable genomic alteration other than KRAS‑G12C; Anti‑HER3 antibody; topoisomerase I inhibitor; antibody‑drug conjugate containing an exatecan derivative; any other anticancer therapy within 14 days of first dose (ignore washout). Biomarkers required: KRAS‑G12C mutation present. Biomarkers excluded: Other actionable driver alterations (e.g., ALK rearrangement, ROS1 fusion, EGFR activating mutation) that have approved targeted therapies.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of other active malignancy within 3 years (exceptions: adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, superficial bladder Ta/Tis/T1, curatively treated in‑situ disease).
Clinically active or symptomatic central nervous system/spinal cord metastasis or leptomeningeal disease (inactive/stabilized lesions permitted under strict criteria).
Unresolved/interfering toxicity from prior anticancer therapy greater than grade 1 (aside from alopecia).
Significant cardiovascular disease (recent MI, NYHA Class II‑IV CHF, unstable angina, serious arrhythmias, LVEF < 50%, prolonged QT/QTc intervals).
Baseline interstitial lung disease, pulmonary fibrosis, or active pneumonitis.
Severe ocular surface or corneal disease.
Concurrent systemic steroids >10 mg prednisone daily or other immunosuppressive therapy.
Active uncontrolled hypertension (>180/>110 mmHg).
Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraception (per gender‑specific reproductive requirements outlined in protocol).
Known uncontrolled HIV infection, active HBV/HCV infection (unless meet stipulated virologic suppression criteria).

Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease defined as second or greater relapse **or** primary refractory disease with at least two prior induction attempts **or** first relapse refractory to at least one prior re‑induction attempt (patients with Down syndrome are **not** eligible). Prior treatment required: CD22 surface expression demonstrated on leukemic blasts. Prior treatment excluded: Inotuzumab ozogamicin **or** prior hematopoietic stem cell transplant **or** Down syndrome. Biomarkers required: CD22 positivity (**and**, if Philadelphia chromosome positive disease, must have had two prior therapy attempts including two different tyrosine kinase inhibitors). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of sinusoidal obstruction syndrome. Isolated central nervous system, testicular, or other extramedullary sites of relapse. Previous treatment with inotuzumab ozogamicin. Current receipt of another investigational drug. Ongoing administration of antitumour agents aside from allowable hydroxyurea or intrathecal chemotherapy. Use of anti‑graft‑versus‑host‑disease or organ‐rejectant prophylaxis (cyclosporine, tacrolimus, etc.). Chronic systemic corticosteroid use outside stipulated exceptions. Known HIV infection, chronic hepatitis B or C infection. Active uncontrolled infection (positive recent bacterial/fungal cultures, febrile illness, active antiviral/protozoal therapy). Concomitant inherited bone‑marrow failure syndromes (Bloom, ataxia‑telangiectasia, Fanconi anaemia, Kostmann, Shwachman‑Diamond, etc.). Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of adequate contraception in persons of reproductive potential.

Prior treatment excluded: Inotuzumab ozogamicin **or** prior hematopoietous stem cell transplant **or** Down syndrome. Biomarkers required: CD22 positivity (**and**, if Philadelphia chromosome positive disease, must have had two prior therapy attempts including two different tyrosine kinase inhibitors). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Known HIV infection, chronic hepatitis B or C infection. Active uncontrolled infection (positive recent bacterial/fungal cultures, febrile illness, active antiviral/protozoal therapy). Concomitant inherited bone‑marrow failure syndromes (Bloom, ataxia‑telangiectasia, Fanconi anaemia, Kostmann, Shwachman‑Diamond, etc.). Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of adequate contraception in persons of reproductive potential.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: any non‑small cell lung cancer histology. Cancer burden allowed: Resectable stage II, stage IIIA, or stage IIIB (N2) disease without pathological complete response after neoadjuvant platinum‑doublet chemotherapy plus pembrolizumab and subsequent surgery. Prior treatment required: neoadjuvant platinum‑based doublet chemotherapy together with pembrolizumab followed by curative intent surgery. Prior treatment excluded: any systemic anticancer therapy other than the specified neoadjuvant platinum‑chemotherapy plus pembrolizumab; any cancer vaccine; any therapy directed at anaplastic lymphoma kinase; any anti‑programmed cell death 1, program­med cell death ligand 1, or program­med cell death ligand 2 agent other than the required neoadjuvant pembrolizumab; any agent targeting CTLA‑4, OX‑40, or CD137. Biomarkers required: EGFR activating mutations (exon 19 deletion or L858R) absent and ALK gene rearrangement absent. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of grade ≥2 pneumonitis or pulmonary fibrosis; uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including symptomatic heart failure or recent myocardial infarction; severe renal impairment (e.g., creatinine clearance <30 mL/min); clinically significant hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin >1.5× ULN or AST/ALT >2.5× ULN); unresolved central nervous system metastasis or leptomeningeal disease; ongoing serious infection including uncontrolled HIV, hepatitis B/C despite therapy; known active autoimmune disease requiring systemic steroids; concurrent malignancy requiring active treatment; pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; ECOG performance status >1; inability to tolerate study procedures or give informed consent.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥1 year. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Primary immunodeficiency‐related Epstein‑Barr virus‑positive lymphoproliferative disorder. Histology allowed: EBV‑positive lymphoid proliferation (any histologic subclass). Cancer burden allowed: Systemic disease alone, systemic plus central nervous system involvement, or central nervous system disease only. Prior treatment required: At least one prior line of systemic therapy and documentation of radiographic disease progression per Lugano classification or lack of complete/partial response to standard first‑line therapy. Prior treatment excluded: History of prior allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplantation or solid organ transplant. Biomarkers required: Demonstration of EBV positivity in tumor tissue (biopsy) or cerebrospinal fluid by validated testing. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of active Burkitt lymphoma, T‑cell lymphoma, natural killer/T‑cell lymphoma, Hodgkin lymphoma, plasmablastic lymphoma, transformed lymphoma, or other malignancy requiring systemic therapy.
Active serious infections, including uncontrolled adenoviral infection or infections necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy at enrollment; known HIV infection.
Grade ≥ 2 acute graft‑versus‑host disease or extensive chronic graft‑vs‑host disease at enrollment.
Requirement for vasopressor or mechanical ventilation support at enrollment.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Inability/unwillingness to follow contraception requirements through day 90 after last dose.
Ongoing corticosteroid (>0.5 mg/kg/day prednisolone-equivalent), methotrexate, or extracorporeal photopheresis (except protocol‑allowed dexamethasone for CNS disease).
Life expectancy less than 60 days or any other life‑threatening illness, organ dysfunction, or condition placing participant at unacceptable risk.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: any solid tumor. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: receipt of all standard therapies appropriate for the individual's disease or documentation of refusal/unavailability of further standard therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: SMARCA4 (BRG1) alteration (any alteration). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Participants with known or likely loss of function alteration of SMARCA2 (BRM) or malignancy associated with SMARCA2 alterations.
Prior exposure to SMARCA2 (BRM) inhibitor(s) and/or degraders (unless specifically permitted).
Untreated or uncontrolled central nervous system involvement.
History of increased risk of prolonged QT interval or clinically significant arrhythmia.
Significant cardiovascular disease.
Active or treated secondary primary malignancy within 2 years prior to enrollment.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, intention to become pregnant or sire a child during study or within 6 months after last dose.
Active autoimmune disease, history of allogeneic stem cell or organ transplant, or compromised immune system within past 2 years (applicable to certain cohort).
