clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥6 months and ≤21 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: atypical teratoid rhabdoid tumor. Histology allowed: atypical teratoid rhabdoid tumor. Cancer burden allowed: any disease status (measurable, evaluable, or absence of detectable disease). Prior treatment required: completion of planned upfront therapy for the disease. Prior treatment excluded: anti‑Programmed Death‑1 agents, anti‑Programmed Death‑Ligand 1 agents, anti‑Programmed Death‑Ligand 2 agents, or anti‑Cytotoxic T‑Lyphocyte Antigen‑4 agents; allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant; solid organ transplant; ezh2 inhibitor therapy (unless patient has relapsed/refractory disease, in which case up to 1 year of prior ezh2 inhibitor is permitted). Biomarkers required: loss of INI1 (confirmed by IHC or bi‑allelic SMARCB1 alteration) **or** loss of SMARCA4 (confirmed by IHC or SMARCA4 alteration). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of HIV infection, chronic hepatitis B or C infection; uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses including active infections; prior non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids or current pneumonitis; active autoimmune diseases requiring systemic therapy within the past 12 months or severe autoimmune syndromes; prior myeloid neoplasms such as myelodysplastic syndrome, AML, or lymphoid leukemias; receipt of prior solid organ transplantation; pregnancy or breastfeeding; prior therapy with any anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2, or anti‑CTLA‑4 agents; administration of live or attenuated vaccinations within 30 days before study start; hypersensitivity reaction to tazemetostat or structurally similar compounds; concomitant participation in another investigational anticancer study; use of strong/moderate CYP3A4 enzyme inducer or inhibitor within 14 days of dosing; inability to comply with study procedures and assessments.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Participants whose disease has transformed to aggressive lymphoma.
Patients with symptomatic or suspected hyperviscosity syndrome or IgM > 4000 mg/dL who cannot undergo plasmapheresis.
Receipt of any other investigational anticancer agent.
Residual toxicities from prior anticancer therapy exceeding Grade 1 (except alopecia and peripheral neuropathy).
Uncontrolled active infection requiring inpatient care or intravenous antimicrobials within 4 weeks of starting treatment.
Unstable cardiovascular disease—including New York Heart Association Class III–IV heart failure, left ventricular ejection fraction < 45 %, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within 6 months, or untreated serious arrhythmia.
Significant central nervous system pathology such as seizures, stroke, psychosis, dementia, cerebral vasculitis, or encephalitis.
Requirement for supplemental oxygen at rest to maintain SpO₂ > 92 %.
Chronic immunosuppressive therapy for non‑study reasons within 28 days, including systemic glucocorticoids ≥ 20 mg/day prednisone‐equivalent.
Known or presumed central nervous system involvement by Waldenström macroglobulinemia (CNS lesions, leptomeningeal disease) or ongoing CNS disease from another malignancy.
Pregnancy, lactation, or inability/unwillingness to comply with mandated contraception measures.
Active alcohol or illicit drug dependence, significant psychiatric illness, or unstable psychosocial circumstance limiting protocol adherence.
History of hypersensitivity reaction to substances chemically or biologically similar to epcoritamab.
Administration of a live or live‑attenuated vaccine within 4 weeks preceding enrollment.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Known central nervous system involvement.
* Active infection requiring systemic antimicrobials at enrollment.
* Major surgery or significant trauma within 4 weeks before starting study medication, or incomplete recovery from such procedure.
* Requirement for chronic warfarin or other vitamin K antagonist anticoagulation.
* Severe inherited or acquired bleeding disorders (e.g., hemophilia, Von Willebrand disease, recurrent spontaneous bleeds).
* Stroke or intracranial hemorrhage within 6 months of first dose of investigational drug.
* Life‑threatening hemorrhage within 3 months of first dose of investigational drug.
* Uncontrolled autoimmune hemolytic anemia or immune thrombocytopenia not attributable solely to the lymphoma.
* Active hepatitis C infection (eligible only if HCV RNA undetectable according to strict assay limits).
* Active hepatitis B infection (eligible only if HBV DNA undetectable and placed on prophylaxis).
* Prior malignant disease unless cured with curative intent and disease free ≥3 years (exceptions: non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ of cervix or breast, Gleason 6 prostate cancer observed).
* Significant cardiovascular disease: NYHA class III/IV heart failure, myocardial infarction within 6 months, unstable angina within 3 months, uncontrolled arrhythmias, ventricular tachyarrhythmias within 6 months, advanced AV block without pacing, uncontrolled hypertension (systolic > 170 mmHg and diastolic > 105 mmHg), resting QTcF > 480 ms.
* inability to swallow capsules or marked gastrointestinal malabsorption.
* concomitant use of strong CYP3A inducer medicines.
* Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during the study period.
* Any severe or uncontrolled comorbidity likely to interfere with safe conduct of the trial or compliance with protocol requirements.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: pathological tumor stage pT1‑3, pathologic nodal stage pN0, no evidence of metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: definitive breast surgery (total mastectomy, skin‑sparring mastectomy, nipple‑sparring mastectomy, or lumpectomy) with adequate margins, axillary staging (sentinel node biopsy, targeted axillary dissection, or axillary lymph node dissection). Prior treatment excluded: systemic chemotherapy, radiation therapy, HER2‑directed therapy, immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy, CDK‑targeted therapy, endocrine therapy lasting >6 weeks for the index cancer, hormonally based contraceptives continued through enrolment. Biomarkers required: estrogen‐receptor positive (≥1 % by IHC) and/or progesterone‑receptor positive (≥1 % by IHC) and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative by ASCO/CAP guideline, Oncotype Dx recurrence score 21‑25 **or** (recurrence score 16‑20 **and** high clinical risk defined as low grade > 3 cm, intermediate grade > 2 cm, or high grade > 1 cm). Biomarkers excluded: HER2 amplification/overexpression.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically evident metastatic disease.
Inflammatory breast cancer or any pT4 lesion.
Previous invasive breast cancer (ipsi­lateral or contra­lateral) excluding pure DCIS or LCIS.
Life expectancy < 10 years owing to comorbidities.
Absolute neutrophil count < 1200/mm³, platelet count < 100,000/mm³, hemoglobin < 10 g/dL, total bilirubin > ULN (or > 1.5×ULN with benign hyperbilirubinemia), ALT/AST > 3×ULN, glomerular filtration rate < 30 ml/min/1.73 m².
Significant cardiovascular disease or NYHA functional classification worse than Class IIIB.
Non‑epithelial breast tumours (sarcoma, lymphoma, etc.).
Any receipt of radiation therapy, chemotherapy, or biological therapy for the current breast cancer prior to enrolment (except brief endocrine exposure ≤6 weeks).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding at baseline; inadequate contraception compliance.
Active untreated chronic hepatitis B infection or detectable hepatitis C viremia.
Uncontrolled central nervous system metastasis.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: non‑squamous non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non‑squamous (predominantly adenocarcinoma). Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: No systemic anticancer therapy for advanced/metastatic disease; prior curative‑intent surgery±radiation permitted; adjuvant or neoadjuvant chemotherapy permissible if completed ≥6 months before diagnosis of advanced/metastatic disease and without disease progression within 6 months after completion. Prior treatment excluded: Any systemic therapy for advanced/metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer; any antibody‑drug conjugate containing a topoisomerase I cytotoxic payload; any TROP2‑targeted therapy; any anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2, anti‑CTLA‑4, anti‑OX40, anti‑CD137, or other immune‑checkpoint inhibitor administered in any setting. Biomarkers required: Programmed death‑ligand 1 tumour proportion score <50% by centralized 22C3 immunohistochemistry; confirmation of absence of actionable genomic alterations (negative for EGFR sensitising mutations, ALK rearrangements, ROS1 fusions, BRAF V600E mutation, MET exon 14 skipping, RET fusions, NTRK fusions, and other FDA‑recognized targetable drivers); availability of adequate tumor tissue for assessment of TROP2 protein expression. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
- Presence of spinal cord compression.
- Active, untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; newly identified brain metastasis without demonstrated radiologic stability for ≥2 weeks off steroids.
- Significant uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (QTcF >470 ms, myocardial infarction ≤6 months, serious arrhythmias, uncontrolled angina ≤6 months, left ventricular ejection fraction <50%, New York Heart Association class II–IV heart failure unless recovered to class I with LVEF ≥50%, uncontrolled hypertension [SBP >180 mmHg or DBP >110 mmHg]).
- Severe concurrent pulmonary pathology (recent pulmonary embolism ≤3 months, severe asthma, severe COPD, restrictive lung disease, substantial pleural effusion, interstitial lung disease, autoimmune/connective‑tissue disease with lung involvement, prior total pneumonectomy).
- Current or past history of grade ≥2 pneumonitis attributable to prior therapy.
- Known human immunodeficiency virus infection, active hepatitis B or C infection.
- Poor functional status (performance status ECOG >2).
- Pregnancy or lactation.
- Receipt of investigational product or biologic (other than standard vaccinations) concurrently with study enrollment.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of grade ≥ 2 peripheral neuropathy.

Previous malignancy other than non‑melanoma skin cancer, in‑situ urothelial, gastric, breast, colorectal or cervical dysplasias, unless in complete remission ≥2 years and off therapy.
Solid organ or allogeneic hematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness—including active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmia, interstitial lung disease, severe chronic diarrheal gastroenteropathy, or untreated psychiatric/socioeconomic factors limiting compliance.
Baseline corrected QT interval (Fridericia) ≥470 milliseconds.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Inadequate performance status (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score > 1).

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant pulmonary toxicity or pneumonitis.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease, including congestive heart failure or myocardial infarction within past year.
Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin > 2× ULN or AST/ALT > 5× ULN).
Severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min) or dialysis dependence.
Active untreated central nervous system infection or meningitis.
Evidence of diffuse leptomeningeal dissemination by imaging.
Brainstem or spinal cord dominant disease involvement.
Ongoing uncontrolled seizure activity despite medication optimization.
Concurrent chronic steroid usage exceeding physiologic replacement doses without stability/decrease over preceding 14 days.
Other active malignant neoplasm requiring ongoing therapy or diagnosed within the last 3 years, excluding adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer or carcinoma in situ.
Known hypersensitivity to safusidenib or any formulation components.
Any medical, psychiatric, or social circumstance that, in investigator’s judgment, would compromise participant safety or adherence to protocol procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: <50. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: osteosarcoma. Histology allowed: osteosarcoma. Prior treatment required: gross complete resection of the primary osseous tumor (performed or planned synchronously with thoracic surgery) and administration of standard systemic chemotherapy consisting of cisplatin combined with doxorubicin or an ifosfamide‑based regimen, delivered presently or completed within the last 60 days. Prior treatment excluded: any therapeutic pulmonary metastasectomy performed before enrollment. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: None.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: <50. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: osteosarcoma. Histology allowed: osteosarcoma. Prior treatment required: completion of standard systemic chemotherapy comprising cisplatin together with doxorubicin or an ifosfamide‑based regimen for the initial primary tumor, finished prior to study entry, and achievement of definitive local control of the primary tumor (surgically resected or adequately managed). Prior treatment excluded: any therapeutic pulmonary metastasectomy performed before enrollment. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: None.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Follicular lymphoma. Histology allowed: Grade 1, Grade 2, Grade 3A, CD20 positive. Cancer burden allowed: Stage II–IV disease AND meets at least one modified GELF criterion (symptomatic adenopathy, organ function impairment due to disease, constitutional symptoms, tumor mass > 7 cm, >3 nodal sites ≥ 3 cm, local compressive symptoms, splenomegaly > 16 cm, clinically significant pleural or peritoneal effusion, leukemic phase > 5×10⁹/L circulating malignant cells, rapid generalized disease progression, renal infiltration, bone lesions). Prior treatment required: None. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic therapy for follicular lymphoma (any chemotherapeutic, targeted, or immune agent). Biomarkers required: CD20 positive (tumor tissue assay). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy (prednisone >10 mg/day) ; patients with bulky cervical adenopathy threatening airway compression; patients who are candidates for curative‑intent radiation therapy; patients who underwent major surgery or suffered significant trauma within four weeks preceding study start; active hepatitis B (HBV DNA PCR positive) or hepatitis C (HCV RNA PCR positive) infection; known HIV seropositivity; active bacterial, viral, fungal, mycobacterial, parasitic, or other infection at enrollment or within two weeks prior; prior/history of another malignancy except non‑melanoma skin cancer or in‑situ cervical or breast carcinoma unless disease‑free ≥2 years; receipt of live attenuated vaccinations within one week prior to entry or during study; severe or uncontrolled concomitant medical illnesses limiting compliance; cardiovascular events within six months (myocardial infarction, congenital long QT syndrome, torsades de pointes, unstable angina, coronary or peripheral arterial bypass graft, grade ≥ 3 arrhythmia, significant electrocardiographic abnormalities, cerebrovascular accident); New York Heart Association class III or IV heart failure or left ventricular ejection fraction < 45%; pregnancy, lactation, or plans to become pregnant during study; prior solid organ or allogenic stem cell transplantation; history of haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis; autoimmune disorders (including myocarditis, pneumonitis, Myasthenia Gravis, myositis, autoimmune hepatitis, systemic lupus erythematosus, rheumatoid arthritis, inflammatory bowel disease, antiphospholipid syndrome-associated thrombosis, Granulomatosis with polyangiitis, Sjögren’s syndrome, Guillain‑Barré syndrome, multiple sclerosis, vasculitis, glomerulonephritis) unless remotely historic and well‑controlled after sponsor discussion; severe hypersensitivity/allergic reaction to anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibodies or any component of epcoritamab; administration of live vaccines within twenty‑eight days prior to first dose; active central nervous system lymphoma; peripheral neuropathy greater than grade 2; baseline corrected QT interval (QTcF) exceeding 470 ms; active SARS‑CoV‑2 infection.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Triple‑negative invasive breast carcinoma (HER2‑negative, ER‑negative, PR‑negative). Cancer burden allowed: Residual invasive disease in breast and/or ipsilateral lymph nodes after completing neoadjuvant chemotherapy, with pathologically clear surgical margins, no distant metastatic disease, and documented presence of circulating tumor DNA in plasma. Prior treatment required: Completed neoadjuvant cytotoxic chemotherapy (standard regimens used for triple‑negative disease) prior to definitive breast and/or axillary surgery; definitive breast surgery (lumpectomy or mastectomy) with clear margins; postoperative radiation when indicated; concomitant bone‑modifying agents (bisphosphonates or RANK‑ligand inhibitors) permitted. Prior treatment excluded: Prior administration of sacituzumab govitecan, irinotecan, or any topoisomerase I‑containing antibody‑drug conjugate for early‑stage disease; any adjuvant (post‑surgical) chemotherapy; prior systemic therapy outside the stipulated neoadjuvant window (excluding allowable immune‑checkpoint inhibitor use in the neoadjuvant setting); prior hypersensitivity to atezolizumab, sacituzumab govitecan, or their excipients. Biomarkers required: HER2 negative (ISH ratio < 2.0 or average HER2 copy number < 4 signals per cell, **or** IHC score 0 or 1+); Estrogen‑receptor negative (**< 10 %**) **and** Progesterone‑receptor negative (**< 10 %**); Detectable circulating tumor DNA harboring tumor‑specific mutations as determined by centralized assay. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis on baseline thoracic imaging. Significant cardiovascular disease including congestive heart failure NYHA class III‑IV, myocardial infarction within six months, unstable angina, or serious uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia. Serious liver disease such as cirrhosis. Uncontrolled active infections—including active tuberculosis—or ongoing systemic antimicrobial therapy (unless prophylaxis). Uncontrolled type I or type II diabetes mellitus (grade ≥ 2). Clinically significant hyperlipidaemia (uncontrolled cholesterol, triglycerides, or calcaemia graded ≥ 2). Gastrointestinal disorders impairing drug absorption (active inflammatory bowel disease, uncontrolled nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea greater than grade 2, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resection). congenital Long QT syndrome or resting QTc > 480 ms. Positive serology for Human Immunodeficiency Virus, Hepatitis B surface Antigen, or Hepatitis C viral RNA. Recent receipt of a live, attenuated vaccine (within four weeks) or anticipated future need for such a vaccine during study participation. Current systemic immunosuppressive therapy (high‑dose glucocorticoids, cyclosporine, tacrolimus, mycophenolate mofetil, biologics targeting TNFα, IL‑6, CD20, etc.) initiated within two weeks preceding study treatment (with minor steroid exemptions noted elsewhere). Pregnancy or breastfeeding; inadequate contraception compliance for women of child‑bearing potential and men. Poor functional status (ECOG performance status > 1).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Any invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable locally recurrent or metastatic disease (Stage IV) with evaluable lesions according to RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: No prior chemotherapy or HER2‑targeted therapy for advanced disease; up to one prior line of endocrine therapy for metastatic disease is permitted; prior (neo)adjuvant chemotherapy and/or HER2‑targeted therapy allowed if disease‑free interval from end of systemic treatment (excluding hormone therapy) to metastatic diagnosis is ≥ 12 months. Prior treatment excluded: Prior exposure to trastuzumab deruxtecan in the adjuvant or neoadjuvant setting; any chemotherapy or HER2‑targeted therapy administered for advanced disease. Biomarkers required: HER2 overexpression/amplification confirmed by centralized laboratory or validated local assay on metastatic or primary tumour specimen; estrogen receptor and progesterone receptor status must be known. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Active uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (unless previously treated and stable).
Concurrent malignancy or malignancy diagnosed within 5 years (exceptions: cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, basal/non‑melanoma skin cancer, Stage I uterine cancer).
Known allergy or hypersensitivity to investigational product components.
Recent palliative radiotherapy (within 2 weeks narrow field or 4 weeks broad field/more than 30 % bone marrow) or major surgery/trauma within 14 days.
Significant cardiovascular disease: recent (<6 months) unstable angina, myocardial infarction, NYHA class II–IV heart failure, left ventricular ejection fraction <55 %, uncontrolled hypertension, symptomatic/permanent arrhythmias, QTc prolongation (>470 ms females, >450 ms males) or congenital Long QT syndromes.
Severe pulmonary comorbidity: recent pulmonary embolism, severe COPD/asthma, restrictive lung disease, extensive pleural effusions, post‑COVID fibrotic changes, prior pneumonectomy.
History of interstitial lung disease / pneumonitis requiring steroids, existing ILD/pneumonitis, or suspicious findings unable to rule out at screening.
Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraception adherence.
Active hepatitis B or C infection (positive PCR), HIV infection, or other active uncontrolled infection.
Receipt of live or attenuated vaccination within 30 days preceding study treatment.
Uncontrolled seizures, ongoing CNS disorders, or serious/unsteady psychiatric illness impairing compliance.
Substance abuse or other severe/chronic medical condition deemed unsuitable by investigators.
Incapacity or refusal to comply with study assessments and follow‑up procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Acute myeloid leukaemia. Histology allowed: Acute myeloid leukaemia. Cancer burden allowed: High risk for post‑transplant relapse defined as any of the following – de novo AML diagnosed at age ≥60 (core‑binding‑factor AML excluded); de novo AML in first complete remission with measurable residual disease positivity by validated flow cytometry; secondary AML; AML transplanted in second complete remission or thereafter; AML with TP53 mutation; therapy‑related AML; AML with monosomy 7; AML with ≥10 % blasts at time of transplant. Prior treatment required: Planned reduced‑intensity conditioning allogeneic haemopoietic stem cell transplant from an HLA‑matched related or related haploidentical donor employing post‑cyclophosphamide GVHD prophylaxis; achievement of morphological complete remission (or CRi) per 2017 ELN criteria. Prior treatment excluded: Prior allogeneic stem cell transplant performed >7 days before intended NK‑cell infusion; receipt of targeted maintenance therapy aimed at FLT3‑ITD, IDH, or BCR‑ABL mutations; use of sirolimus in GVHD prophylaxis; systemic glucocorticoids >10 mg prednisone daily on day of NK infusion. Biomarkers required: Either TP53 mutation (allows enrolment without measurable residual disease) **or** measurable residual disease positivity (required for de novo AML in CR1 cohort). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of untreated or progressive central nervous system/leukemic involvement (extramedullary disease in sanctuary sites such as CNS or testes).
Uncontrolled concurrent illnesses including active serious infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmia, or major psychiatric/social impairment limiting adherence.
Pregnancy or currently breastfeeding.
Known HIV infection.
Active and uncontrolled hepatitis B or C virus infection.
Significant hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin >1.5× ULN, ALT/AST >3× ULN) despite allowances noted elsewhere.
Renal insufficiency (creatinine >2.0 mg/dL) outside permissible limits.
Poor performance status (ECOG >2/KPS <60%).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Any hematologic malignancy treated with allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation. Histology allowed: Acute lymphoblastic leukemia, acute myeloid leukemia, chronic myeloid leukemia, Hodgkin lymphoma, Non‑Hodgkin lymphoma, Myelodysplastic syndrome, other bone‑marrow derived neoplasms. Cancer burden allowed: Newly diagnosed or incipient bronchiolitis obliterans syndrome occurring after allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplantation that meets National Institutes of Health diagnostic criteria (FEV₁/VC < 0.70 or <5ᵗʰ %predicted, FEV₁ < 75 %predicted with ≥10 %absolute decline over ≤2 years, absent active infection, plus imaging or physiologic support feature) **OR** fulfills atypical BOS criteria (FEV₁ < 80 %predicted with ≥10 %decline over 2 years, VC < 80 %predicted, FEV₁/VC ≥ 0.7, absent active infection/non‑infectious lung disease). Prior treatment required: Received an allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior therapy specifically administered for bronchiolitis obliterans syndrome; prior exposure to belumosudil; concurrently receiving any other investigational immunosuppressive agent for chronic graft‑versus‑host disease. Biomarkers required: Documented negative HIV test; no active hepatitis B virus infection (negative HBsAg and undetectable HBV DNA) and no active hepatitis C virus infection (undetectable HCV RNA). Biomarkers excluded: Positive HIV infection; active hepatitis B or C infection requiring treatment or at risk for reactivation.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant pneumonitis unrelated to BOS. Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including congestive heart failure NYHA class III-IV or recent myocardial infarction. Baseline left ventricular ejection fraction <45%. Severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min) or dialysis dependence. Grade 3–4 transaminase elevation (>5×ULN) persisting despite optimal care. Untreated/uncontrolled central nervous system involvement or symptomatic brain metastases. Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals. Inability to give informed consent or comply with study procedures. Concurrent systemic anticancer therapy intended for underlying malignancy. Recent (<28 days) major surgery or radiation therapy affecting lungs.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of severe brain injury or stroke.
Unable to undergo scheduled sequential MRI examinations.
Any seizure disorder unrelated to the tumor.
Recent use of strong cytochrome P450 3A4 inhibitors or inducers (within 2 weeks).
Concurrent use of other seizurogenic medications.
Past interstitial lung disease, drug‑induced ILD, or radiation pneumonitis requiring steroids.
Severe or uncontrolled comorbid conditions (e.g., severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease).
Prior exposure to pneumotoxic chemotherapy leading to pulmonary toxicity.
Creatine kinase >5 times upper limit of normal on two consecutive screens.
Cardiac dysfunction (myocardial infarction ≤6 months, NYHA class II–IV heart failure, unstable angina, or serious arrhythmias).
Active severe pulmonary infection (including tuberculosis, uncontrolled hepatitis B/C in Japan cohort).
Unresolved toxicities greater than NCI CTCAE grade 1 (aside from alopecia); persistent grade 2 toxicities permissible only under limited conditions.
Prior receipt of an ATM/ATR inhibitor combined with radiation therapy.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Participant has known active central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis (treated/stable brain metastasis acceptable under stability criteria).
Concurrent administration of other cancer‐specific therapy or investigational agent during the study.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness—including active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, or cardiac arrhythmia.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Presence of another malignancy that is progressing or requires active treatment (exceptions noted for cured skin cancers, in‑situ cervical cancer, low‑risk prostate cancer on active surveillance; remote remission ≤2 years permissible).
Significant existing autoimmune disorders (exception made for stable hypothyroidism due to Hashimoto’s).
Long‑term (>4 weeks) systemic corticosteroid use exceeding prednisolone/prednisone 10 mg/day (or equivalent).
Major surgical procedure or serious trauma within 4 weeks preceding enrollment or planned within 4 weeks post‑first dose.
History of bleeding tendency, coagulopathy, or clinically significant hemorrhage within 4 weeks (includes gastrointestinal bleed, hemoptysis ≥½ tsp blood, significant epistaxis, therapeutic anticoagulation within 14 days).
Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic ≥150 mmHg or diastolic ≥100 mmHg despite therapy).
Recent (<12 months) major cardiovascular events (unstable angina, MI, NYHA class II⁺ CHF, significant peripheral artery disease, stroke/TIA, arterial/venous thromboembolism grade III+) .
Recent (<6 months) severe gastro‑intestinal pathology (ulcers, variceal bleeding, fistulas, obstructions, major resections) or hepatic/biliary complications.
Acute exacerbation of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease within 4 weeks.
Radiologic evidence of major blood‑vessel invasion/encasement by tumour or intratumoral cavitation.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of allergy to tumor necrosis factor α, lomustine, related excipients, or intravenous human proteins/peptides/antibodies. Baseline absolute neutrophil count < 1.5 × 10⁹/L, platelet count < 100 × 10⁹/L, hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dL. Creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min or serum creatinine > 1.5 times upper limit of normal. Alanine aminotransferase, aspartate aminotransferase, alkaline phosphatase ≥ 2.5 × ULN or total bilirubin ≥ 2.0 × ULN. International normalized ratio > 1.5 × ULN. Significant cardiovascular conditions including heart failure greater than NYHA class II, recent myocardial infarction or unstable angina, clinically significant arrhythmias, left ventricular ejection fraction < 55 %, prolonged QT/QTc interval, uncontrolled hypertension, known arterial aneurysm at high risk of rupture, ischemic peripheral vascular disease grades IIb–IV. Active or historical autoimmune disease deemed liable to worsen with immune stimulation. Cerebrovascular event within the preceding year. Psychiatric illnesses such as major depression, bipolar disorder I or II, OCD, schizophrenia, suicide attempts or ideations, homicide ideation, or severe personality disorders; anxiety grade ≥ 3. Advanced diabetic retinal disease (severe non‑proliferative or proliferative retinopathy). Major traumatic injury or major surgery within four weeks before dosing. Current or prior tuberculosis infection. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Ongoing high‑dose corticosteroid or immunosuppressive therapy outside permitted limits. Active, uncontrolled infections or other severe comorbidities posing excessive risk. Concurrent malignant neoplasms unless disease‑free for at least two years. Receipt of live vaccines within four weeks prior to treatment or planned receipt during the study. Therapeutic anticoagulation with P2Y12 inhibitors or vitamin K antagonists. Non‑healing wounds, ulcers, or fractures. Requirements for additional investigational anticancer agents alongside study medication.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of inflammatory breast cancer (clinical T4d).
Severe cardiac disease including congestive heart failure NYHA class III or greater, symptomatic coronary artery disease, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, significant arrhythmias (ventricular tachyarrhythmia, ventricular fibrillation, sustained supraventricular tachycardia), syncope of cardiovascular cause, or documented sudden cardiac death.
Significant pulmonary disorders such as interstitial lung disease, severe dyspnea at rest, dependence on supplemental oxygen, or other severe respiratory illness.
Uncontrolled infectious processes: active systemic bacterial infection requiring IV antibiotics, active fungal infection, or clinically evident viral infection (HIV positivity, active hepatitis B or C).
Renal insufficiency with estimated glomerular filtration rate less than 30 mL/minute.
Baseline gastrointestinal toxicity of grade 2 or higher (e.g., ongoing Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, or other causes of frequent watery stools).
Any other concomitant serious disease judged by the investigator to jeopardize safety or protocol compliance.

Prior treatment required: Surgical or chemical castration with continued androgen‑deprivation therapy resulting in castrate testosterone levels; progression on at least one androgen‑receptor directed therapy (enzalutamide **or** abiraterone **or** apalutamide **or** darolutamide); receipt of exactly one taxane chemotherapy regimen in the metastatic castration‑resistant setting (evidence of one prior taxane exposure in mCRPC, additional taxanes prohibited); Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status 0 or 1. general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias (sustained VT, second‑ or third‑degree AV block without pacing), congenital Long QT/Torsade de Pointes predisposition, uncontrolled hypertension, symptomatic coronary artery disease, recent myocardial infarction, New York Heart Association class II-IV heart failure, moderate/severe valvular disease, impaired left ventricular systolic function, chronic kidney disease stage IV/V or creatinine clearance <30 ml/min, Child-Pugh Class C liver disease, untreated/uncontrolled central nervous system metastases, active viral hepatitis (HBV, HCV) or HIV infection, active autoimmune disorder requiring systemic steroids, unresolved grade ≥2 toxicities from prior anticancer therapy, pregnancy or breastfeeding (not applicable to males but retained as standard clause), inability to comply with study procedures, receipt of any experimental drug within 4 weeks preceding enrollment, and any medical, psychiatric, or social circumstance judged by investigators to place subject at undue risk or compromise compliance.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Systemic light‑chain (AL) amyloidosis. Histology allowed: Light‑chain amyloid deposits. Cancer burden allowed: Cardiac stage I, II, or IIIa per 2013 European‑modified Mayo Clinic staging (Stage IIIB excluded). Prior treatment required: Prior therapy containing daratumumab together with cyclophosphamide, bortezomib, and dexamethasone (daratumumab + CyBorD). Prior treatment excluded: Any prior BCMA‑targeting bispecific antibody AND any prior BCMA‑directed chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy; refractoriness to belantamab mafodotin; receipt of belantamab mafodotin as the immediate prior line of therapy. Biomarkers required: Differential free light chain increase >20 mg/L indicating progressive disease AND measurable hematologic disease defined as (involved minus uninvolved free light chain >40 mg/L **or** abnormal absolute free light chain level with abnormal κ/λ ratio, except in CKD stage ≥3 where a λ free light chain rise ≥50 % over nadir to abnormal level with normal κ/λ ratio is accepted **or** serum M‑protein spike ≥0.5 g/dL). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of allergic reaction to elranatamab. Active concomitant malignant neoplasm (with limited exceptions for appropriately treated skin cancers, low‑risk prostate cancer, or other indolent/localized cancers). Current pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during study period and up to four months post‑treatment. Incomplete recovery from prior anticancer therapy related toxicities exceeding Grade 1 (aside from alopecia). Concurrent enrollment in another interventional investigation for AL amyloidosis. Uncontrolled autonomic hypotension causing syncope/presyncope despite optimal therapy. Significant untreated infectious diseases including active HIV infection, uncontrolled hepatitis B or C infections. Clinically meaningful autoimmune disorders. Recent (<3 months) acute coronary syndromes or unstable ventricular arrhythmias. Severely reduced left ventricular ejection fraction (<30%). Severe valvular stenosis (valve area <1.0 cm²) or critical congenital heart defects. Documented life‑threatening ventricular tachyarrhythmias absent appropriate device support. Prolonged corrected QT interval (>550 ms) without paced rhythm. Acute myocardial ischemia or dangerous conduction disturbances on baseline electrocardiography (beyond isolated first‑degree AV block, Mobitz I second‑degree AV block, BBB patterns, stable AF with controlled rate, or benign bifascicular blocks). Planned major surgical procedure requiring general anaesthesia within 4 weeks of registration or anticipated during study course. New York Heart Association functional classification IV or acutely decompensated congestive heart failure. Intent to proceed with autologous stem‑cell transplantation within six months of starting study medication. Poor performance status beyond stipulated ECOG/Karnofsky limits. Renal insufficiency with creatinine clearance <30 ml/min, hepatobiliary enzyme elevations surpassing protocol-specified multiples of upper limits, or cytopenias falling below mandated minimum blood counts. Any additional medical, psychiatric, or psychosocial factor judged by investigators to compromise subject safety or compliance.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: renal cell carcinoma. * Any other malignant neoplasm within 3 years preceding enrolment, or residual disease from a prior malignancy (exceptions: carcinoma in situ of the cervix, non‑melanoma skin cancer, ductal carcinoma in situ, stage I uterine cancer, or other cancers with ≥90% 5‑year overall survival).  
* Active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (treated/stable brain metastases permitted if clinically stable ≥4 weeks, off steroids/anticonvulsants ≥7 days).

* Frequent therapeutic paracenteses (>approximately once every 4 weeks) for symptomatic ascites (indwelling peritoneal catheters subject to investigator discretion).  
* Uncontrolled concurrent illness such as significant cardiac, pulmonary, hepatic, or renal impairment; active infection including HIV or viral hepatitis; or Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status greater than acceptable limits (typically >2).

general_exclusion_criteria:
- Presence of smoldering multiple myeloma, plasma cell leukemia, POEMS syndrome, or AL amyloidosis.  
- Recent autologous or allogeneic stem cell transplant within twelve weeks or active graft‑versus‑host disease.  
- Active hepatitis B, hepatitis C, SARS‑CoV‑2, HIV, or any untreated bacterial, fungal, or viral infection.  
- Significant cardiovascular disease occurring within six months (acute myocardial infarction, unstable angina, severe arrhythmias, thromboembolic or cerebrovascular event, prolonged QT interval, etc.).  
- Another active malignancy diagnosed within three years, except adequately treated basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, or carcinoma in situ.  
- Grade ≥ 2 persistent peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy; history of Guillain–Barré syndrome or Grade ≥ 3 peripheral motor polyneuropathy.  
- Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraceptive measures.  
- Known hypersensitivity to elranatamab or its excipients.  
- Concurrent enrolment in another interventional study for multiple myeloma.  
- Administration of a live attenuated vaccine within four weeks of starting study medication.  
- Unresolved toxicities from prior anticancer therapy greater than grade 1 (except alopecia and stable peripheral neuropathy).  
- Major surgery or invasive procedures performed within fourteen days of enrollment.  
- Serious underlying medical or psychiatric disorders (including recent suicide attempts or behavior) deemed unsuitable by the investigators.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of active interstitial pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Primary or acquired immunodeficiency that is poorly controlled or requires ongoing immunosuppressive therapy.
Uncontrolled intercurrent medical illnesses (infection, serious autoimmune disorder, significant cardiac arrhythmia, unstable angina, major GI disorders impairing pill ingestion).
Systemic corticosteroid use exceeding 10 mg prednisolone-equivalent daily (≥18 y) or >0.5 mg/kg (≤18 y) or other immunosuppressants within 14 days prior to enrolment.
Peripheral neuropathy greater than grade 1 or known Charcot‑Marie‑Tooth disease.
Concurrent or recent malignancy that could confound safety/efficacy assessment.
Live vaccinations administered within 30 days preceding Day 1 of protocol therapy.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Inability or unwillingness to use adequate contraception throughout treatment and designated post‑treatment periods.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Myocardial infarction or unstable angina within six months prior to screening.
Pleural effusion requiring therapeutic drainage within twenty‑eight days prior to screening.
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy or uncontrolled despite treatment.
Untreated brain metastases.
Poor performance status (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score greater than 1).
Organ dysfunction exceeding protocol-specified laboratory thresholds (hepatic, renal, hematologic).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥70 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Prior treatment required: documented disease progression on prior endocrine therapy **or** prior cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor (palbociclib or ribociclib) **or** prior chemotherapy. general_exclusion_criteria: Major surgery within 14 days before study drug initiation or incomplete recovery from postoperative complications. Current use of prohibited concomitant medications that cannot be stopped seven days prior to study drug administration. Participation in another investigational therapeutic trial. Receipt of additional antitumor agents aside from prescribed study regimens. Concurrent immune‑suppression agents other than allowable corticosteroids (e.g., systemic cytochrome P450 modulators such as cyclosporine or tacrolimus) when deemed risky. Use of herbal supplements potentially interacting with study drug pharmacokinetics. Known allergy to any components of abemaciclib formulation. Active systemic bacterial infection necessitating IV antibiotic therapy, active fungal infection, or demonstrable viral infection—including HIV positivity or active hepatitis B/C. Significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing drug absorption (e.g., inflammatory bowel disease flare, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting/diarrhea, extensive resections). Cardiovascular histories comprising unexplained syncopal episodes, pathologic ventricular arrhythmias, sustained ventricular tachycardia/fibrillation, or witnessed sudden cardiac death. Any other severe or uncontrolled medical illness judged to pose undue safety risk or impede adherence (examples: chronic pancreatitis, uncontrolled hepatic disease). Inability to ingest oral tablets. Severe pulmonary pathology such as interstitial lung disease, resting hypoxemia, or dependence on supplemental oxygen. Renal insufficiency with calculated creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min. Recent major gastric or intestinal surgeries affecting digestion/absorption. Pre‑existent moderate-to-severe diarrhea (baseline Grade ≥ 2). Documented non‑adherence to medical regimens. Presence of a second malignant neoplasm diagnosed within two years preceding enrolment, except satisfactorily treated basal/squamous cell cutaneous carcinomas, non‑melanoma skin cancers, or surgically cured cervical cancer.

Prior treatment required: 4–10 cycles of carboplatin plus paclitaxel plus pembrolizumab; candidate for continued maintenance pembrolizumab. Prior treatment excluded: Prior CDK4/6 inhibitor therapy (e.g., abemaciclib). Biomarkers required: ER‐positive (≥1% nuclear staining) **and** TP53 wild‑type **and** mismatch repair proficient **and** tumor mutational burden <10 mutations/​Megabase (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: TP53‑mutated **or** mismatch repair‑deficient **or** tumor mutational burden ≥10 mutations/​Megabase.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease. Congestive heart failure or left ventricular systolic dysfunction. Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5×ULN, transaminases >3×ULN). Moderate-to-severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min). Untreated or unstable central nervous system involvement, including active brain metastases. Known HIV infection, active hepatitis B or C infection. Performance status worse than ECOG 2. Current untreated systemic bacterial, fungal, or viral infection requiring antimicrobial therapy. Pregnant or breastfeeding persons. Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inducers or inhibitors. Clinically meaningful cardiovascular abnormalities (syncope of cardiac cause, sustained ventricular arrhythmias, sudden cardiac death). Presence of another invasive malignancy diagnosed within the past three years (excluding adequately treated cervical carcinoma in situ, basal cell skin carcinoma, or DCIS of the breast).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: Squamous. Cancer burden allowed: Completely resected stage IIA, IIB, IIIA or IIIB (including pathologic T3‑4 N2) non small cell lung cancer with negative margins (R0). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Neoadjuvant or adjuvant systemic therapy for the current lung cancer; allogeneic solid organ or tissue transplant. Biomarkers required: Programmed death ligand‑1 immunohistochemistry performed using an FDA‑cleared assay (DAKO 22C3, DAKO 28‑8, EIL3N or SP263). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of infectious pneumonia or non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroid treatment. Current or unresolved pulmonary toxicity attributable to prior therapy. Symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmias, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within six months. Significant renal impairment (creatinine clearance <45 mL/min) or hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin >1.5× ULN, AST/ALT >2.5× ULN). Untreated or progressive central nervous system metastases. Known human immunodeficiency virus infection with detectable viremia, chronic hepatitis B surface antigen positivity, or active hepatitis C RNA detection. Ongoing systemic glucocorticoid (>10 mg prednisone equivalent daily) or other immunosuppressive medication usage. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Recent administration (<30 days) of live attenuated vaccines. Severe allergic reaction (grade ≥ 3) to pembrolizumab or its components. Poor functional status (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status > 1).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: Non squamous. Biomarkers excluded: Epidermal growth factor receptor exon 19 deletion or L858R mutation; Anaplastic lymphoma kinase rearrangement.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of untreated, symptomatic, or radiographically progressive brain metastasis; active autoimmune or inflammatory disorder; myocardial infarction occurring ≤ six months before randomisation; past or ongoing non‑infectious pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease or suspicion thereof; concurrent severe pulmonary comorbidity interfering with study assessments; known contraindication to standard platinum‑based doublet chemotherapy or immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy; inadequate organ function outside protocol specifications; Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status > 1.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: squamous cell carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: no prior systemic therapy for recurrent or metastatic disease (patients may have received systemic therapy >6 months prior as part of adjuvant or definitive treatment for locoregionally advanced disease). Prior treatment excluded: prior anti‑transforming growth factor‑beta therapy; prior anti‑epidermal growth factor receptor antibody therapy (exceptions: use limited to radiosensitisation in multimodal locoregional treatment). Biomarkers required: PD‑L1 combined positivity score ≥ 1 (PD‑L1 IHC 22C3 pharmDx assay performed at screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Disease suitable for curative-intent local therapy; progressive disease ≤6 months after completing curative-intent systemic therapy for locoregionally advanced disease; life expectancy <3 months; known active central nervous system metastases, spinal cord compression, carcinomatous meningitis, or leptomeningeal disease; current active major bleeding or major bleeding event within 4 weeks preceding enrollment; participation in another interventional study without adequate washout; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy within the past 2 years; chronic hepatitis B infection not controlled on antivirals; untreated or actively replicating hepatitis C infection; known HIV infection; receipt of any solid-organ or hematopoietic stem-cell transplantation involving ongoing immunosuppression; diagnosis/treatment of another malignant neoplasm within 2 years (exemptions: cured basal/squamous skin cancer, in‑situ cervix, in‑situ breast, low‑risk early-stage prostate cancer); concurrent systemic glucocorticoids (>10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent) or other immunosuppressants within 7 days of first dose (topical/intranasal/etc. exempt); administration of live or live‑attenuated vaccines within 4 weeks prior to screening.

general_exclusion_criteria: Inability to undergo magnetic resonance imaging of the brain; active systemic disease; uncontrolled intercurrent illness; prior malignancies requiring active treatment except adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin carcinoma, in situ cervical cancer, superficial bladder cancer, or other cancers disease‑free ≥3 years; inability to safely ingest oral medication or gastrointestinal conditions impairing absorption; known human immunodeficiency virus infection, prior progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy, or any active serious infection (bacterial, viral, fungal); hypersensitivity or anaphylactic reactions to paxalisib or its constituents; concomitant use of strong cytochrome P450 3A4 inhibitors or inductors affecting paxalisib metabolism; existing interstitial lung disease, severe resting dyspnoea, or dependence on supplemental oxygen; underlying Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, or chronic disorders producing baseline grade 2 or greater diarrhoea; untreated or poorly controlled type I diabetes, or type II diabetes with haemoglobin A1c > 9 % or fasting glucose > 140 mg/dL on two measurements within 14 days; uncontrolled hypertension despite optimized therapy; hepatitis B core antibody–positive /surface antigen–negative individuals lacking confirmatory negative PCR, hepatitis B surface antigen–positive persons, hepatitis B/DNA-positive cases, hepatitis C antibody–positive individuals without subsequent negative RNA PCR, or hepatitis C RNA‑positive cases; current pregnancy or lactation; simultaneous participation in another therapeutic clinical trial.

New or active brain metastasis.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: uveal melanoma. Histology allowed: melanoma of the uvea. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic therapy in the metastatic or advanced setting; any prior regional or liver‑directed therapy; prior exposure to a protein kinase C (PKC) inhibitor (including IDE196); prior exposure to a mesenchymal– epithelial transition factor (MET) inhibitor; prior exposure to a guanine nucleotide binding protein subunit alpha Q/GNAS family member (GNAQ/11) inhibitor. general_exclusion_criteria: History of interstitial lung disease, active pneumonitis, or past pneumonitis; symptomatic or untreated central nervous system metastases or CNS metastases requiring corticosteroids; concurrent malignant disease; adverse events from prior anticancer therapy not resolved to Grade ≤ 1; high risk of syncope; known acquired immunodeficiency syndrome-related illness or active hepatitis B or C infection; active adrenal insufficiency, active ulcerative colitis, Crohn’s disease, or other inflammatory bowel disease; active infections necessitating systemic antibacterial therapy; use of hematopoietic colony‑stimulating factors prior to initiation of study drug; current pregnancy or lactation in women; severe hypersensitivity reaction histories to biological products; contraindications to any investigator’s‐choice comparator according to labeling; cerebrovascular accident (stroke) occurring within six months preceding first dose; any additional medical, psychiatric, or social condition judged by the investigator to increase risk or compromise data integrity.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed disease after ≥ one prior line of therapy and satisfies 2019 IWCLL criteria indicating need for treatment. Prior treatment required: At least one prior antileukemic regimen (e.g., chemo‐immunotherapy, monoclonal antibodies). Prior treatment excluded: Any prior covalent Bruton's Tyrosine Kinase inhibitor (e.g., ibrutinib, acalabrutinib) and any prior B‑cell lymphoma 2 inhibitor (e.g., venetoclax). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of BTK C481X mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation. Central nervous system involvement by disease. Concurrent enrolment in another interventional investigational study. Other malignant neoplasms unless cured >2 years ago, adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or low‑risk prostate cancer on active surveillance. Administration of live, attenuated vaccines within 4 weeks preceding cycle 1 day 1. Uncontrolled active systemic infection or recent serious bacterial infection requiring IV antibiotics within 7 days. Clinically significant bleeding diathesis (e.g., Von Willebrand disease, hemophilia). Stroke, intracranial haemorrhage, or major bleed within past 6 months. Requirement for warfarin or other Vitamin K antagonist anticoagulation. Concomitant use of moderate or strong CYP3A inhibitors or inducers deemed unacceptable. Positive serology for HIV, active Hepatitis B or C infection. Hypersensitivity to zanubrutinib, venetoclax, or structurally related compounds. Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraception. Significant gastrointestinal malabsorption syndromes, extensive resections, obstructive pathology, or inflammatory bowel disease compromising drug uptake. Current medically unmanaged cardiovascular disease—including uncontrolled arrhythmia, NYHA class III–IV heart failure, recent (<6 months) myocardial infarction, unstable angina, or acute coronary syndrome. Poor functional status or organ dysfunction jeopardising safety (ECOG > 2 despite allowance here, though higher grades noted elsewhere). Ongoing autoimmune hemolytic anaemia or immune-mediated thrombocytopaenia."""

Positive serology for HIV, active Hepatitis B or C infection. Hypersensitivity to zanubrutinib, venetoclax, or structurally related compounds. Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraception. Significant gastrointestinal malabsorption syndromes, extensive resections, obstructive pathology, or inflammatory bowel disease compromising drug uptake. Current medically unmanaged cardiovascular disease—including uncontrolled arrhythmia, NYHA class III–IV heart failure, recent (<6 months) myocardial infarction, unstable angina, or acute coronary syndrome. Poor functional status or organ dysfunction jeopardising safety (ECOG > 2 despite allowance here, though higher grades noted elsewhere). Ongoing autoimmune hemolytic anaemia or immune-mediated thrombocytopaenia."""

general_exclusion_criteria:
Concurrent participation in another therapeutic clinical trial.
Symptomatic brain metastases or symptomatic leptomeningeal disease.
History of prior invasive cancer (other than fully excised cutaneous squamous/basal cell carcinoma or in‑situ carcinoma removed >2 years ago).
Major surgical procedure within 4 weeks before study start; radiotherapy (unless palliative for bone pain) within 2 weeks before entry; palliative radiotherapy (>10 fractions) must have ended ≥48 hours earlier.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease within 6 months (myocardial infarction, unstable angina, recent CABG/PAG, NYHA class II or higher heart failure, stroke/TIA, serious arrhythmia, ongoing antiarrhythmic drugs).
Resting QTcF > 470 ms or other clinically important electrocardiographic abnormality; medications or conditions predisposing to QT prolongation.
Active infectious disease—including bacterial, fungal, viral infections and HIV positivity.
Significant gastrointestinal disorders affecting drug absorption (Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, short bowel syndrome, severe malabsorptive conditions).
Peripheral sensory neuropathy grade ≥ 2.
Moderate-to-severe interstitial lung disease or fibrotic lung disease (history of pneumonitis, idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, radiation‑induced pneumonitis grades 3‑4).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Major bleeding event or grade >3 arrhythmia occurring on prior BTK inhibitor therapy.
Female participants who are pregnant, nursing, or plan to become pregnant or breastfeed during study participation or within six months after the last dose.
Men who are unwilling to use a latex condom consistently with a partner of childbearing potential throughout study participation and for six months thereafter.
Presence of central nervous system lymphoma.
Documented human immunodeficiency virus infection.
Active cytomegalovirus infection.
Positive hepatitis B surface antigen or hepatitis B DNA positivity; anti‑core antibody-positive individuals with confirmed HBV PCR positivity.
Positive hepatitis C antibody together with detectable hepatitis C RNA.
Concurrent use of warfarin.
Concurrent use of moderate or strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers, or strong P‑glycoprotein inhibitors.
Ongoing systemic immunosuppressive therapy exceeding equivalent of prednisone <20 mg/day.
Receipt of live, attenuated vaccinations within four weeks before first study dose.
Active uncontrolled bacterial, viral, fungal, or parasitic infection (excluding superficial fungal nail infection).
Major surgery performed within four weeks prior to initiation of study drug.
Conditions causing malabsorption or otherwise prohibiting oral drug delivery.
History of alcohol or illegal drug misuse.
Incapacity to swallow capsules/pills or inability to attend scheduled outpatient visits and lab draws.
Active uncontrolled autoimmune cytopenias (autoimmune hemolytic anemia, ITP) necessitating therapeutic change within four weeks before enrolment.
Corrected QT interval (Fridericia) >470 ms on at least two of three sequential ECGs during screening.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease—including unstable angina, myocardial infarction within six months, left ventricular ejection fraction ≤45 % within twelve months, NYHA class III‑IV heart failure, or uncontrolled symptomatic arrhythmias.
Any other substantial coexisting illness, medical condition, surgical history, physiologic finding, ECG anomaly, or laboratory abnormality deemed by the investigator to jeopardize patient safety, pharmacokinetics/pharmacodynamics of study drugs, or integrity of data collection.
Known hypersensitivity to any excipient contained in pirtobrutinib or venetoclax formulations.
Historical pattern of non‑adherence to prescribed medical regimens.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: systemic light chain amyloidosis. Histology allowed: Congo red-positive amyloid deposits with green birefringence confirming AL amyloidosis and identifiable clonal plasma cell source. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after at least one prior line of anti‑plasma cell therapy; cardiac involvement staged I, II, or IIIa by the 2013 European modification of the Mayo clinic staging system (NT‑proBNP < 8500 pg/mL and appropriate troponin thresholds); plasma cell infiltration ≤60% in bone marrow with no lytic bone lesions or CRAB end‑organ damage (bone lesion negative, hypercalcemia negative, renal insufficiency attributable to amyloid, anemia not solely due to plasma cell clone); measurable disease defined by serum or urinary M‑protein ≥500 mg/dL or serum free light chain ≥20 mg/L with abnormal κ/λ ratio or differential free light chain ≥20 mg/L. Prior treatment required: at least one prior line of anti‑plasma cell therapy (any regimen). Prior treatment excluded: prior treatment with ixazomib citrate (MLN9708) or prior treatment with venetoclax. Biomarkers required: presence of chromosomal translocation t(11;14) demonstrated by fluorescent in situ hybridisation on bone marrow specimen. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Recent major surgery or radiotherapy within 14 days (7 days if localized field); anti‑plasma cell therapy within 4 weeks (excluding nitrosourea or mitomycin C exceptions); unrecovered grade >1 toxicities from prior therapy (aside from alopecia); participation in another investigational study within 30 days; central nervous system involvement; hypersensitivity to venetoclax, ixazomib citrate, dexamethasone, or structurally related compounds; contraindicated coadministration of strong or moderate CYP3A inhibitors or inducers, or potent P‑gp modulators; uncontrolled comorbidities including active infection within 14 days, active hepatitis B or C infection, uncontrolled hypertension, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant arrhythmias, myocardial infarction within six months; psychiatric or social circumstances compromising protocol adherence; current lactation or positive pregnancy test; gastrointestinal disease or procedures impeding oral drug absorption; peripheral neuropathy grade ≥3 or grade 2 with painful symptoms; New York Heart Association class III/IV; advanced cardiac amyloidosis Mayo stage IIIB (NT‑proBNP > 8500 pg/mL); grade 3 or higher diarrhea.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukaemia; any inherited bleeding disorder; persistent severe cytopaenia; uncontrolled systemic illness or active untreated infection; refractory nausea/vomiting, chronic gastrointestinal disease impairing drug absorption, inability to swallow tablets, or extensive prior bowel resection; prior unrelated malignant neoplasm; lingering grade ≥ 2 adverse events attributable to former anticancer therapy (aside from alopecia); symptomatic spinal cord compression, brain metastasis, carcinomatous meningitis, or leptomeningeal disease; active, unmanaged hepatitis B or C infection; active, unmanaged HIV infection; active tuberculosis; clinically significant cardiac arrhythmia or serious cardiovascular disease; ongoing exogenous reproductive hormone therapy or non‑topical hormonal therapy for non‑oncological reasons; major surgery or substantial trauma within four weeks preceding first dose or expected need for major surgery during study; recent palliative radiotherapy covering >30 % of bone marrow within four weeks or confined-field radiotherapy within two weeks before first dose; receipt of systemic anticancer therapy for locoregional recurrence or metastatic disease outside protocol allowances; transfusion of blood products or administration of colony‑stimulating factors within 28 days before enrolment; concurrent systemic anticancer medication; use of strong or moderate cytochrome P450 3A4 inducers/inhibitors, sensitive CYP2B6 substrates, or narrow‑therapeutic‑index CYP2C9/CYP2C19 substrates (e.g., warfarin, phenytoin) within five half‑lives of randomisation; usage of drugs known to markedly prolong QT interval or cause torsades de pointes; systemic atropine use.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of active hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or human immunodeficiency virus infection.; Active uncontrolled infection.; Recent (within 6 months) myocardial infarction, unstable angina, New York Heart Association class ≥ II heart failure, life‑threatening arrhythmias, stroke or transient ischemic attack.; Prolonged corrected QT interval (>450 ms for males, >470 ms for females).; Clinically active central nervous system leukemia.; Ongoing systemic immunosuppressive therapy post‑hematopoietic stem cell transplant without appropriate washout.; Grade > 2 acute graft‑versus‑host disease, moderate/severe limited chronic GVHD, or extensive chronic GVHD.; Concomitant use of additional chemotherapeutic or anti‑leukemic agents aside from permissible intrathecal chemotherapy, hydroxyurea for rapid proliferation/count control, or corticosteroids for differentiation syndrome.; Severe gastrointestinal or metabolic disorders impairing oral drug absorption.; Current active malignant neoplasm requiring treatment.; Pregnancy or breastfeeding.; Uncontrolled medically significant comorbidities or psychiatric illnesses compromising safety or protocol compliance.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: pathology‑confirmed node‑positive disease restricted to pelvic lymph nodes (external/internal iliac and/or obturator nodes) with no distant metastatic disease (M0). Prior treatment required: radical prostatectomy (any surgical approach); detectable postoperative serum prostate‑specific antigen >0 ng/mL measured ≥30 days after surgery. Prior treatment excluded: androgen deprivation therapy administered before radical prostatectomy; any prior systemic chemotherapy directed at prostate cancer; prior radiotherapy to the prostate/prostate fossa region that would overlap future radiation fields; prior treatment with any androgen‑receptor signaling inhibitor (e.g., abiraterone acetate, enzalutamide, apalutamide, darolutamide); current use of a 5‑alpha reductase inhibitor. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of seizure or conditions predisposed to seizure (including recent stroke, cerebral vascular malformations, intracranial neoplasms); serious cardiovascular events within 12 months (unstable angina, myocardial infarction, arterial or venous thrombosis, major arrhythmia) or NYHA class III–IV heart failure; baseline moderate-to-severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh B or C); inadequate bone marrow reserve (hemoglobin <9 g/dL, platelets <100 × 10⁹/L); creatinine clearance <30 mL/min; elevated liver enzymes >2.5 × ULN (AST/ALT) or bilirubin >1.5 × ULN; uncontrolled hypertension (systolic ≥160 mmHg or diastolic ≥100 mmHg); active untreated infection; gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral medication absorption; inability to swallow oral capsules/tablets; ongoing use of systemic steroids exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone equivalent; inflammatory bowel disease; pregnancy or breastfeeding; plans to enroll concurrently in another interventional therapeutic trial.

general_exclusion_criteria: History or current diagnosis of impaired cardiac function, clinically significant cardiovascular disease, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, congestive heart failure, cardiomyopathy, symptomatic pericarditis, coronary artery bypass graft within six months, long QT syndrome, familial sudden death syndromes, resting QTcF ≥450 ms (men) or ≥460 ms (women), left ventricular ejection fraction <50%; uncontrolled arterial hypertension (systolic ≥160 mmHg or diastolic ≥100 mmHg); severe hepatic impairment (alanine aminotransferase or aspartate aminotransferase >3× upper limit of normal, total bilirubin >1.5× ULN except Gilberts’ variant limits); inadequate hematologic reserve (absolute neutrophil count <1.5 ×10⁹/L, platelet count <100 ×10⁹/L, hemoglobin <10 g/dL); reduced renal function (creatinine clearance <60 ml/min); chronic pancreatitis or unexplained elevation of serum lipase >1.5× ULN deemed clinically significant; ongoing infections requiring systemic antibiotics, human immunodeficiency virus infection, active hepatitis B or C infection; history of another malignant neoplasm within three years (except non‑melanoma skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, or carcinoma in situ cured); pregnant or lactating women; inability to tolerate study procedures or give informed consent.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥12 months and ≤21 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma. Histology allowed: High‑grade glioma (anaplasia, glioblastoma, HGG NOS) or unconfirmed (clinical/radiographic diagnosis sufficient). Cancer burden allowed: Localized disease confined to the pons, no metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Surgical procedure (biopsy or partial/subtotal resection) and/or glucocorticoids permitted. Prior treatment excluded: Systemic chemotherapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy, radiation therapy, or any other anti‑cancer agents administered before enrollment. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients must not have received any prior therapy for their central nervous system malignancy except surgery and steroids. Current receipt of another investigational drug or any other anti‑cancer agents disqualifies entry. Uncontrolled infections are prohibited. History of solid organ transplantation excludes participants. Grade greater than 1 extrapyramidal movement disorders are excluded. Known macular degeneration, uncontrolled glaucoma, or significant cataracts lead to exclusion. Evidence of metastatic disease eliminates eligibility. Inability to undergo prescribed radiation therapy results in exclusion. Pregnant women are ineligible; lactating women are excluded unless they agree not to breastfeed; females of childbearing potential must demonstrate a negative pregnancy test and commit to adequate contraceptive measures throughout the study and for 90 days post‑therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥12 months and ≤21 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma (atypical presentation). Histology allowed: Biopsy‑proven high‑grade glioma (anaplastic astrocytoma, glioblastoma, HGG NOS) with documented H3 K27M mutation. Cancer burden allowed: Localized disease confined to the pons, no metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Diagnostic biopsy/surgery and/or glucocorticoids permitted. Prior treatment excluded: Systemic chemotherapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy, radiation therapy, or any other anti‑cancer agents administered before enrollment. Biomarkers required: H3 K27M mutation detected and BRAF V600 wild type and IDH1 wild type. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of BRAF V600 mutation or IDH1 mutation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non‑squamous. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic (stage IV) disease, not amenable to curative surgery or radiation; measurable disease per RECIST 1.1; brain metastases permissible if previously treated and currently stable. Prior treatment required: None specified. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic therapy for metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer (including chemotherapy, targeted therapy, or immunotherapy). Biomarkers required: Presence of a tumor mutation in STK11 **or** KEAP1 **or** KRAS (single or co‑mutation). Biomarkers excluded: Absence of activating epidermal growth factor receptor mutations **and** absence of ALK fusions.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of acute or uncontrolled illness or allogeneic organ transplantation. Mixed small cell lung cancer and non‑small cell lung cancer histology. Major surgical procedures within 28 days preceding study drug administration or planned during study. Active or prior documented autoimmune or inflammatory disorders requiring systemic immunosuppression (exceptions noted for vitiligo, alopecia, stable hypothyroidism, dietary-controlled celiac disease, mild chronic skin conditions). Contraindications to platinum‑based doublet chemotherapy. Other current malignant disease unless cured with no activity for ≥2 years (excluding adequately resected non‑melanoma skin cancer and in‑situ disease). Persistently severe toxicities (grade ≥2) attributable to previous anticancer therapy, aside from allowable grades ≤2 neuropathy at investigator’s discretion. Unstable spinal cord compression. Clinically significant cardiovascular disease (symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, uncontrolled serious arrhythmias, etc.). Ongoing concurrent antitumour chemotherapy, investigational agent, biological or hormonal therapy (except hormonally indicated for non‑oncological reasons). Disallowed radiation therapy (unless definitive radiation completed ≥6 months prior, or approved limited palliative irradiation). Untreated or progressive central nervous system metastasis; unrepaired leptomeningeal carcinomatosis. Positive testing for active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection. Active hepatitis B surface antigen positivity, unresolved hepatitis C viremia, or uncontrolled HIV infection. Recent use (within 14 days) of systemic immunosuppressive medications beyond stipulated exemptions. Administration of live attenuated vaccine within 30 days prior to dosing. Enrollment in another interventional clinical trial concurrently. Hypersensitivity to study drugs or excipients. Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraception in individuals capable of becoming pregnant.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma, IDH-wildtype, World Health Organization grade 4 (including gliosarcoma). Histology allowed: IDH-wildtype glioblastoma or gliosarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent or progressive disease with ≤2 prior relapses; measurable disease according to RANO criteria; candidate for surgical resection; tumor involvement of cortical gray matter; no diffuse leptomeningeal disease; no extracranial disease. Prior treatment required: prior radiotherapy with or without chemotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to troriluzole or riluzole. Biomarkers required: IDH1/2 wildtype demonstrated by DNA sequencing in a CLIA/CAP–certified laboratory; availability of archival formalin‑fixed paraffin‑embedded tumor tissue for additional sequencing. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
ANC count <1500/mm³.
Platelet count <100,000/mm³.
Hemoglobin <9 g/dL.
Total bilirubin >2×upper limit of normal (except permissible in Gilberts’ syndrome).
Aspartate transaminase >1.5×ULN.
Alanine transaminase >1.5×ULN.
Serum creatinine >1.5 mg/dL or estimated CrCl <60 mL/min.
Positive serum ß‑hCG in individuals assigned female at birth who are of child‑bearing potential.
Laboratory abnormalities meeting institutional limits for safe study conduct.
History of interstitial lung disease.
Active hepatitis B, HIV infection, or untreated hepatitis C infection.
Severe, acute, or chronic medical or psychiatric conditions compromising safety or data integrity.
Uncontrolled active infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Significant allergic reaction or unresolved toxicities greater than Grade 1 related to prior therapy.
Major surgery within 28 days preceding study drug initiation.
Inability to tolerate magnetic resonance imaging.
Use of strong CYP1A2 inhibitors or inducers, anti‑glutamatergic agents, or hepatotoxic drugs that cannot be discontinued appropriately.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced, unresectable, or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: No more than three prior systemic cytotoxic chemotherapy regimens (including antibody-drug conjugates) for locally advanced/metastatic breast cancer; documented disease progression on or after standard‐of‑care therapies or intolerance to them; if BRCA mutated, must have received a poly (ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitor when available and not contraindicated; for hormonally responsive (estrogen or progesterone receptor positive) tumors, must have progressed on ≥2 lines of endocrine therapy for locally advanced/metastatic disease and have received a cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor in the adjuvant or metastatic setting, OR progressed on 1 line of endocrine therapy with relapse while on adjuvant endocrine therapy after definitive surgery and have received a CDK4/6 inhibitor in the adjuvant or advanced setting; for hormonal receptor‑negative, HER2‑low, PD‑L1‑positive (combined proportion score ≥10) tumors, must have received pembrolizumab (or another PD‑(L)1 inhibitor) combined with chemotherapy when available and not contraindicated. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: HER2‑low expression defined as immunohistochemistry 1⁺ or immunohistochemistry 2⁺ with nonamplified in situ hybridization; additionally, either estrogen/progestogen receptor positivity (for HR⁺ group) or PD‑L1 CPS ≥ 10 (for HR⁻ group) as indicated by respective subgroup criteria. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Known hypersensitivity to any excipients in disitamab vedotin or tucatinib.
Prior therapy with antibody‑drug conjugates bearing monomethyl auristatin E (MMAE) payload.
Prior therapy with tucatinib.

Diagnosed type 1 diabetes mellitus or inadequately controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus (insulin-requiring or HbA₁c outside protocol limits).
Hypersensitivity to FX‑909 or any formulation excipient.
Any gastrointestinal disorder interfering with tablet ingestion or absorption.
Immediate family members employed by the sponsor or investigative site personnel (unless special IRB exemption granted).
Psychosocial, geographic, or logistical circumstances compromising adherence to study procedures.
