clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: NA. Prior treatment required: enrollment in a parent pembrolizumab‑containing study with current administration of pembrolizumab, a pembrolizumab‑based combination, or lenvatinib. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: interstitial lung disease.
general_exclusion_criteria:
* Severe grade ≥ 3 hypersensitivity reaction to pembrolizumab or any excipient.
* Receipt of a live vaccine within 30 days prior to first dose of second‑course phase treatment.
* Diagnosis of immunodeficiency or chronic systemic glucocorticoid (>10 mg/day prednisolone equivalents) or other immunosuppressive therapy within 7 days prior to day 1 of cycle 1.
* Presence of a concurrent actively progressing malignancy requiring treatment (exceptions: cured early‑stage cancers, non‑ulcerated thin melanomas, basal/squamous cell skin cancers, in‑situ cervical or breast cancer).
* Known active central nervous system metastasis or leptomeningeal disease.
* Active autoimmune disease needing systemic therapy within the preceding 2 years (physiological hormone replacements exempted).
* History of non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids or current pneumonitis.
* Active infection necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy.
* Positive serology or documented history of HIV infection.
* Positive serology or documented history of hepatitis B or hepatitis C (unless permissible per parent protocol).
* Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or cause conception during study conduct and until 120 days post‑last dose.
* Significant cardiovascular disease (arrhythmias requiring chronic medication, NYHA class III/IV heart failure, symptomatic ischemic heart disease).
* Hepatic decompensation (Child‑Pugh total score > 6, classes B or C).
* Uncontrolled thyroid disorder.
* Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus.
* Previous allogenic hematopoietic stem cell or solid organ transplantation.
* Current or historical active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection.
Additional exclusions applicable to participants receiving lenvatinib:
* Major surgery performed ≤ 3 weeks before first dose of study drug.
* Pre‑existent grade ≥ 3 gastro‑intestional or nongastrointestinal fistulas.
* Urinary protein excretion ≥ 1 g/24 h.
* Left ventricular ejection fraction below institutionally accepted lower limit.
* Radiologic evidence of major vessel encasement/invasion or intra‑tumour cavitation.
* Corrected QT interval (Fridericia) > 480 ms.
* Clinically significant cardiovascular events within 12 months (NYHA class III/IV CHF, unstable angina, MI, cerebrovascular accident, hemodynamically compromising arrhythmia).
* Conditions impairing oral absorption of lenvatinib (e.g., marked GI malabsorption).
* Active haemoptysis (≈ ½ teaspoon bright red blood) within 3 weeks prior to first dose.
* Hypersensitivity to any component of lenvatinib.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Bladder, ureter, renal pelvis, or urethral urothelial carcinoma. general_exclusion_criteria: Severe cutaneous toxicity (Grade 4) or rash involvement > 30 % BSA.; Underlying physical or psychiatric condition rendering adherence improbable.; Personal history of chronic dermatoses (e.g., eczema, psoriasis) or recurring skin infections that could confound assessments.; Baseline laboratory abnormalities: absolute neutrophils < 1000/mm³, white blood cells < 3000/mm³, platelets < 50,000/mm³, AST > 2.5× upper limit of normal, ALT > 2.5× ULLN, bilirubin > 1.5× ULLN, creatinine > 1.5× ULLN.; Requirement for concurrent radiation therapy to head, neck, or upper torso, or receipt of radiation to those sites within 4 weeks before baseline.; Recent administration (< 4 weeks) of aprepitant or other neurokinin‑1 receptor antagonists.; Participation in another interventional investigational product study within 4 weeks preceding enrollment (or ≥ 8 half‑lives of the agent).; Active untreated infection or uncontrolled comorbidity aside from the target malignancy.; Concomitant medications known to strongly inhibit or induce CYP3A4 administered within 30 days before baseline (e.g., diltiazem, ketoconazole, rifampin, carbamazepine, etc.).; Known hypersensitivity to aprepitant or any component of the study gel formulation.; Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or plans to conceive during the study period.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: classical Hodgkin lymphoma. Histology allowed: classical Hodgkin lymphoma (World Health Organization classification), excluding nodular lymphocyte predominant Hodgkin lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after ≤1 prior line of systemic therapy (radiotherapy not counted); measurable disease defined as at least one fluorodeoxyglucose‐positron emission tomography–avidity lesion ≥1.5 cm in longest diameter; not a candidate for autologous stem‑cell transplantation. Prior treatment required: Receipt of ≤1 prior systemic anticancer regimen; patients may have received brentuximab vedotin or an anti‑programmed death‑1/programmed death ligand‑1 agent as part of frontline therapy only if they achieved a complete metabolic response and have not relapsed within six months after completion of frontline therapy. Prior treatment excluded: receipt of >1 prior systemic therapy line. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Concomitant investigational therapy; live vaccination within 30 days before first dose of study drugs; grade ≥ 2 peripheral neuropathy; prior grade ≥ 3 hypersensitivity to brentuximab vedotin or nivolumab; active central nervous system involvement by lymphoma; history of another malignant neoplasm not in remission for ≥3 years (exception for non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ of cervix, or tumors unlikely to require treatment for ≥2 years); systemic corticosteroid or immunosuppressive therapy exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent within 14 days of study drug initiation (unless inhaled/topical or physiologic adrenal replacement); history of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy; inherited or acquired immunodeficiency; active pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; active, known, or presumed autoimmune disease needing systemic therapy (limited exemptions apply); current hepatitis B or C infection meeting virologic criteria (patients with resolved HBV or cured HCV may enroll under stipulated conditions); untreated HIV infection (controlled HIV with CD4 ≥ 200 permissible); recent major cardiovascular events—including cerebrovascular accident, unstable angina, myocardial infarction—or NYHA Class III‑IV congestive heart failure within 6 months; pregnancy or lactation; inability to comply with study procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: any malignant breast epithelial neoplasm. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic or unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: ≥ one prior systemic therapy in the advanced/metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: previous receipt of neratinib or DS‑8201a. Biomarkers required: her2 positivity demonstrated by ANY ONE OF the following – HER2 immunohistochemistry score 3⁺ (according to ASCO–CAP breast guidelines) **or** HER2 amplification by in situ hybridisation (IS​H) satisfying a HER2:CEP17 ratio ≥ 2 or an average HER2 copy number ≥ 6 per cell (according to ASCO–CAP breast guidelines) **or** a known activating HER2 mutation (e.g., G309A/E, S310F/Y, …, L869R, H878Y, all EGFR exon 20‑like insertions listed). Biomarkers excluded: NA
general_exclusion_criteria: History of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis; clinically severe pulmonary compromise (e.g., COPD, asthma, pulmonary embolism ≤ 3 months, restrictive lung disease, prior pneumonectomy); recent major surgery or radiation within 4 weeks (thoracic radiation restricted); chronic systemic corticosteroid or immunosuppressant use outside AE management; concurrent use of potent CYP3A4 or P‑gp modulators; myocardium infarction within 6 months or symptomatic congestive heart failure (NYHA II–IV); prolonged QTc interval (> 470 ms females, > 450 ms males); clinically significant ocular surface disease; untreated or draining pleural/pericardial/ascitic effusions; spinal cord compression; uncontrolled infectious process needing intravenous antimicrobial therapy; unresolved toxicity from preceding anticancer therapy (grade > 1); active substance/alcohol dependence impairing compliance; pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraception adherence; prior allo‑stem cell or organ transplant.

Inadequate renal function, hepatic dysfunction, or clinically significant hematologic abnormalities
